meanings.marathi: जो या आत्म्याला मारणारा समजतो किंवा जो याला मरतो असे मानतो, त्या दोघांनाही ज्ञान नाही; कारण हा आत्मा कोणाला मारत नाही आणि कोणाकडून मारला जात नाही.

core_meaning: The soul is transcendental to the acts of killing and being killed
use_cases: removing the ego of being a 'doer' or 'victim'
resolving guilt associated with unavoidable professional actions
philosophical reflection on the nature of life and death
developing a perspective beyond the physical plane
embedding_text: soul neither slays nor is slain, slayer vs slain, ignorance of the soul's nature, transcendental self, beyond material violence

sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः । अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥ २० ॥
transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā kadācin nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ ajo nityaḥ śāśvato ’yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre

meanings.english: For the soul there is neither birth nor death at any time. He is unborn, eternal, ever-existing and primeval. He is not slain when the body is slain.
meanings.hindi: आत्मा के लिए किसी भी समय न जन्म है न मृत्यु। वह अजन्मा, नित्य, शाश्वत और पुरातन है। शरीर के मारे जाने पर भी वह नहीं मारा जाता।
meanings.hinglish: Soul ka na kabhi birth hota hai na death. Wo hamesha se hai aur hamesha rahega. Sharir ke marne par bhi soul nahi marti.
meanings.marathi: आत्म्याचा कधीही जन्म होत नाही आणि कधी मृत्यूही होत नाही. तो अजन्मा, नित्य, शाश्वत आणि पुरातन आहे. शरीराचा नाश झाला तरी त्याचा नाश होत नाही.

core_meaning: The ultimate declaration of the soul's immortality and beginningless nature
use_cases: the ultimate comfort in the face of mortality
finding an anchor in a world of constant change
embedding_text: soul neither born nor dies, unborn eternal ever-existing, not slain when body is slain, ajo nitya sasvato, ultimate immortality of soul

sanskrit: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् । कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥ २१ ॥
transliteration: vedāvināśinaṁ nityaṁ ya enam ajam avyayam kathaṁ sa puruṣaḥ pārtha kaṁ ghātayati hanti kam

meanings.english: O Partha, how can a person who knows that the soul is indestructible, eternal, unborn and immutable kill anyone or cause anyone to kill?
meanings.hindi: हे पार्थ! जो व्यक्ति यह जानता है कि आत्मा अविनाशी, अजन्मा, शाश्वत और अपरिवर्तनीय है, वह भला किसे मार सकता है या किसके द्वारा मारा जा सकता है?
meanings.hinglish: Arjun, jo insaan ye samajhta hai ki soul amar hai aur kabhi khatam nahi ho sakti, wo bhala kisi ko kaise maar sakta hai?
meanings.marathi: हे पार्था, ज्याला हे माहित आहे की आत्मा अविनाशी, नित्य, अजन्मा आणि अव्यय आहे, तो मनुष्य कोणाला मारणार किंवा कोणाकडून मारला जाणार?

core_meaning: Knowledge of the soul's immortality removes the ego-driven guilt of being a 'killer'
use_cases: resolving moral dilemmas regarding difficult duties
understanding that the core essence is beyond physical harm
overcoming the 'doer' complex in intense situations
rationalizing necessary actions for the sake of justice
developing a deeper philosophical view on life and death
embedding_text: soul is indestructible and unborn, how can one kill, overcoming the ego of killing, eternal nature of self, Krishna's logic on violence for justice

sanskrit: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि । तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥ २२ ॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsi jīrṇāni yathā vihāya navāni gṛhṇāti naro ’parāṇi tathā śarīrāṇi vihāya jīrṇāny anyāni saṁyāti navāni dehī

meanings.english: As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.
meanings.hindi: जैसे मनुष्य पुराने कपड़ों को त्याग कर नए कपड़े धारण करता है, वैसे ही आत्मा पुराने और व्यर्थ शरीरों को त्याग कर नए शरीर धारण करती है।
meanings.hinglish: Jaise hum purane kapde badal kar naye pehente hain, waise hi soul purani body chhod kar nayi body me chali jaati hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याप्रमाणे माणूस जुने वस्त्रे सोडून नवीन वस्त्रे परिधान करतो, त्याचप्रमाणे आत्मा जुने शरीर सोडून नवीन शरीरात प्रवेश करतो.

core_meaning: Death is merely a change of 'clothing' for the eternal soul
use_cases: the most iconic verse for grieving and loss
removing the 'finality' and horror associated with death
understanding the continuous journey of the individual
finding hope in the concept of rebirth and new beginnings
visualizing the soul as distinct from the physical form
embedding_text: soul changing bodies like clothes, vasamsi jirnani, reincarnation, death is just a transition, accepting the end of a physical body

sanskrit: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः । न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥ २३ ॥
transliteration: nainaṁ chindanti śastrāṇi nainaṁ dahati pāvakaḥ na cainaṁ kledayanty āpo na śoṣayati mārutaḥ

meanings.english: The soul can never be cut to pieces by any weapon, nor burned by fire, nor moistened by water, nor withered by the wind.
meanings.hindi: आत्मा को न शस्त्र काट सकते हैं, न आग जला सकती है, न जल भिगो सकता है और न ही वायु सुखा सकती है।
meanings.hinglish: Soul ko na talwar kaat sakti hai, na aag jala sakti hai, na pani bhigo sakta hai aur na hawa sukha sakti hai. Ye indestructible hai.
meanings.marathi: या आत्म्याला शस्त्रे तोडू शकत नाहीत, अग्नी जाळू शकत नाही, पाणी भिजवू शकत नाही आणि वारा वाळवू शकत नाही.

core_meaning: The soul is transcendental to all physical elements and material forces
use_cases: finding inner strength when facing physical threats
realizing the spiritual self is beyond material destruction
meditation on the indestructible nature of 'I'
overcoming fear of accidents or elemental disasters
establishing the priority of the spirit over matter
embedding_text: soul cannot be cut or burned, transcendental to elements, invulnerability of the soul, weapons cannot harm the self, spiritual security

sanskrit: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च । नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥ २४ ॥
transliteration: acchedyo ’yam adāhyo ’yam akledyo ’śoṣya eva ca nityaḥ sarva-gataḥ sthāṇur acalo ’yaṁ sanātanaḥ

meanings.english: This individual soul is unbreakable and insoluble, and can be neither burned nor dried. He is everlasting, present everywhere, unchangeable, immovable and eternally the same.
meanings.hindi: यह आत्मा अखंडनीय, अदह्य, अक्लेद्य और अशोष्य है। यह नित्य, सर्वव्यापी, स्थिर, अचल और सनातन है।
meanings.hinglish: Soul unbreakable hai, ye kabhi badalti nahi, har jagah hai aur hamesha se aisi hi hai.
meanings.marathi: हा आत्मा तोडला जाऊ शकत नाही, जाळला जाऊ शकत नाही, भिजवला जाऊ शकत नाही आणि वाळवलाही जाऊ शकत नाही. तो नित्य, सर्वव्यापी, स्थिर, अचल आणि सनातन आहे.

core_meaning: Scientific and philosophical properties of the soul as an eternal constant
use_cases: understanding the soul's characteristics in detail
finding a sense of 'permanent home' within oneself
realizing the self exists beyond the limits of space and time
combating the feeling of being 'broken' or 'diminished' by life
embedding_text: soul is unbreakable and insoluble, everlasting and all-pervading, sthanur acalo yam sanatanah, permanent identity, spiritual essence

sanskrit: अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते । तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि ॥ २५ ॥
transliteration: avyakto ’yam acintyo ’yam avikāryo ’yam ucyate tasmād evaṁ viditvainaṁ nānuśocitum arhasi

meanings.english: It is said that the soul is invisible, inconceivable and immutable. Knowing this, you should not grieve for the body.
meanings.hindi: यह आत्मा अदृश्य, अकल्पनीय और अपरिवर्तनीय कही जाती है। ऐसा जानकर तुम्हें शरीर के लिए शोक नहीं करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Soul ko dekha nahi ja sakta, ye soch se pare hai aur badalti nahi. Jab ye sach pata hai, toh rona kaisa?
meanings.marathi: हा आत्मा अव्यक्त, अचिंत्त्य आणि अविकारी आहे असे म्हटले जाते. म्हणून हे जाणून घेऊन तू शोक करणे योग्य नाही.

core_meaning: The soul is beyond material perception, hence grief based on physical loss is illogical
use_cases: accepting things that cannot be 'seen' or 'measured' by science
stopping the habit of lamenting over physical changes
trusting in the 'invisible' core of being
embedding_text: soul is invisible and inconceivable, immutable nature, do not grieve, avyakto yam acintyo, soul beyond material logic

sanskrit: अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् । तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैनं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥ २६ ॥
transliteration: atha cainaṁ nitya-jātaṁ nityaṁ vā manyase mṛtam tathāpi tvaṁ mahā-bāho nainaṁ śocitum arhasi

meanings.english: If, however, you think that the soul will always be born and die forever, you still have no reason to lament, O mighty-armed.
meanings.hindi: यदि तुम यह भी मानते हो कि आत्मा का बार-बार जन्म होता है और बार-बार मृत्यु होती है, तो भी हे महाबाहु! तुम्हें शोक करने का कोई कारण नहीं है।
meanings.hinglish: Agar tum ye bhi socho ki life bas aati jaati rehti hai aur soul permanent nahi hai, tab bhi dukh karne ki koi baat nahi hai.
meanings.marathi: आणि जर तू या आत्म्याला सतत जन्मणारा आणि सतत मरणारा मानत असशील, तरीही हे महाबाहो, तू शोक करणे योग्य नाही.

core_meaning: Even from a purely material or cyclic perspective, constant lamentation is futile
use_cases: finding peace even if one doesn't fully grasp 'immortality'
realizing that constant change is the only constant
reducing emotional drama over natural life cycles
applying practical logic to emotional distress
embedding_text: even if soul is always born and dies, no reason to lament, logical approach to death, addressing materialists, mahabaho sarcasm

sanskrit: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च । तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥ २७ ॥
transliteration: jātasya hi dhruvo mṛtyur dhruvaṁ janma mṛtasya ca tasmād aparihārye ’rthe na tvaṁ śocitum arhasi

meanings.english: One who has taken his birth is sure to die, and after death one is sure to take birth again. Therefore, in the unavoidable discharge of your duty, you should not lament.
meanings.hindi: जिसका जन्म हुआ है उसकी मृत्यु निश्चित है, और मृत्यु के बाद जन्म निश्चित है। इसलिए जो अटल है, उसके लिए तुम्हें शोक नहीं करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Jo paida hua hai wo marega hi, aur jo mara hai wo phir paida hoga. Ye life ka circle hai jise koi badal nahi sakta, toh phir dukh kyun?
meanings.marathi: ज्याचा जन्म झाला आहे त्याचा मृत्यू निश्चित आहे आणि ज्याचा मृत्यू झाला आहे त्याचा जन्म निश्चित आहे. म्हणून या अपरिहार्य गोष्टीबद्दल तू शोक करणे योग्य नाही.

core_meaning: The inevitability of the life-death cycle makes grief for the unavoidable illogical
use_cases: focusing on duty when faced with unavoidable tragedy
reducing the shock of loss by understanding its inevitability
embedding_text: death is certain for the born, birth is certain for the dead, unavoidable duty, do not lament the inevitable, cycle of life and death

sanskrit: अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत । अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥ २८ ॥
transliteration: avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārata avyakta-nidhanāny eva tatra kā paridevanā

meanings.english: All created beings are unmanifest in their beginning, manifest in their interim state, and unmanifest again when annihilated. So what need is there for lamentation?
meanings.hindi: सभी प्राणी जन्म से पहले अप्रकट थे, बीच में प्रकट होते हैं, और मृत्यु के बाद फिर से अप्रकट हो जाते हैं। फिर ऐसी स्थिति में शोक कैसा?
meanings.hinglish: Hum pehle bhi nahi dikhte the, baad me bhi nahi dikhenge, bas beech me thode waqt ke liye dikhte hain. Is temporary cheez ke liye rona kaisa?
meanings.marathi: सर्व प्राणी जन्मापूर्वी अव्यक्त होते, मध्यंतरी व्यक्त होतात आणि मृत्यूनंतर पुन्हा अव्यक्त होतात. मग त्याबद्दल शोक कशाला?

core_meaning: Physical manifestation is just a brief interval in an eternal timeline
use_cases: understanding the temporary nature of human presence
reducing attachment to the physical form
seeing life as a brief appearance in an eternal context
coping with 'existential dread' by normalizing the unmanifest state
viewing life and death as transitions between states
embedding_text: unmanifest beginning and end, manifest middle, temporary appearance of beings, no need for lamentation, states of existence

sanskrit: आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनामाश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः । आश्चर्यवच्चैनं अन्यः शृणोति श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥ २९ ॥
transliteration: āścarya-vat paśyati kaścid enam āścarya-vad vadati tathaiva cānyaḥ āścarya-vac cainam anyaḥ śṛṇoti śrutvāpy enaṁ veda na caiva kaścit

meanings.english: Some look on the soul as amazing, some describe him as amazing, and some hear of him as amazing, while others, even after hearing about him, cannot understand him at all.
meanings.hindi: कोई इस आत्मा को आश्चर्य से देखता है, कोई आश्चर्य से इसका वर्णन करता है, और कोई इसे आश्चर्य से सुनता है; जबकि कोई इसके बारे में सुनकर भी इसे बिल्कुल नहीं समझ पाता।
meanings.hinglish: Koi soul ko dekh kar hairaan hota hai, koi iske baare me bata kar hairaan hota hai, par bahut log sunne ke baad bhi kuch samajh nahi paate.
meanings.marathi: कोणी या आत्म्याला आश्चर्याने पाहतो, कोणी आश्चर्याने त्याचे वर्णन करतो आणि कोणी आश्चर्याने ऐकतो; परंतु ऐकूनही कोणालाही तो समजत नाही.

core_meaning: The soul is a profound mystery that remains beyond average comprehension
use_cases: acknowledging the limits of human intellect
approaching spiritual study with a sense of awe
understanding why 'self-realization' is a rare achievement
normalizing the difficulty in grasping metaphysical concepts
encouraging persistent inquiry into the nature of the self
embedding_text: soul as amazing and wonderful, inconceivable mystery, difficulty in understanding the soul, awe-inspiring nature of the self

sanskrit: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत । तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥ ३० ॥
transliteration: dehī nityam avadhyo ’yaṁ dehe sarvasya bhārata tasmāt sarvāṇi bhūtāni na tvaṁ śocitum arhasi

meanings.english: O descendant of Bharata, he who dwells in the body can never be slain. Therefore you need not grieve for any living being.
meanings.hindi: हे भरतवंशी! शरीर में रहने वाला यह आत्मा कभी नहीं मारा जा सकता। इसलिए तुम्हें किसी भी प्राणी के लिए शोक करने की आवश्यकता नहीं है।
meanings.hinglish: Sharir ke andar jo soul hai, usey kabhi maara nahi ja sakta. Isliye kisi bhi living being ke liye rona band karo.
meanings.marathi: हे अर्जुना, सर्वांच्या शरीरात राहणारा हा आत्मा नित्य अवध्य आहे. म्हणून तू कोणत्याही प्राण्याबद्दल शोक करणे योग्य नाही.

core_meaning: Conclusion of the metaphysical argument: the eternal dweller is safe, so grief is misplaced
use_cases: establishing a firm boundary between soul and body
summarizing the fundamental spiritual lesson on immortality
releasing generic anxiety about the welfare of others
embedding_text: dweller in the body cannot be slain, do not grieve for any being, final conclusion on soul's immortality, shift from soul-knowledge to duty

sanskrit: स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि । धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाच्छ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥ ३१ ॥
transliteration: sva-dharmam api cāvekṣya na vikampitum arhasi dharmyād dhi yuddhāc chreyo ’nyat kṣatriyasya na vidyate

meanings.english: Considering your specific duty as a kshatriya, you should know that there is no better engagement for you than fighting on religious principles; and so there is no need for hesitation.
meanings.hindi: अपने क्षत्रिय धर्म का विचार करते हुए तुम्हें हिचकिचाना नहीं चाहिए, क्योंकि एक क्षत्रिय के लिए धर्म के लिए युद्ध करने से बढ़कर अन्य कोई कल्याणकारी मार्ग नहीं है।
meanings.hinglish: Apne duty (Svadharma) ko dekho, ek warrior ke liye sahi vajah se ladne se badhkar kuch nahi hai. Isliye darna ya hichkichana band karo.
meanings.marathi: आपल्या स्वधर्माचा विचार केला तरी तू डगमगणे योग्य नाही, कारण क्षत्रियासाठी धर्मयुद्धापेक्षा अधिक श्रेयस्कर दुसरे काहीही नाही.

core_meaning: Fulfilling one's inherent social and moral duty (Svadharma) is the highest path to progress.
use_cases: understanding the importance of professional ethics
finding motivation in one's specific role in society
embedding_text: kshatriya duty, svadharma, fighting for principles, no hesitation in duty, religious warfare, professional responsibility

sanskrit: यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् । सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥ ३२ ॥
transliteration: yadṛcchayā copapannaṁ svarga-dvāram apāvṛtam sukhinaḥ kṣatriyāḥ pārtha labhante yuddham īdṛśam

meanings.english: O Partha, happy are the kshatriyas to whom such fighting opportunities come unsought, opening for them the doors of the heavenly planets.
meanings.hindi: हे पार्थ! वे क्षत्रिय सुखी हैं जिन्हें युद्ध के ऐसे अवसर अपने आप प्राप्त होते हैं, जो उनके लिए स्वर्ग के द्वार खोल देते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Arjun, wo warriors khushnaseeb hote hain jinhe aisa mauka apne aap milta hai, ye toh swarg jane ka khula rasta hai.
meanings.marathi: हे पार्था, ज्या क्षत्रियांना असे युद्ध आपोआप प्राप्त होते, ते खरोखर भाग्यवान आहेत, कारण हे त्यांच्यासाठी स्वर्गाचे उघडलेले द्वारच आहे.

core_meaning: Unsolicited challenges should be viewed as rare opportunities for growth and elevation.
use_cases: understanding that 'hard' paths can lead to 'high' rewards
rethinking bad luck as a spiritual or professional 'open door'
embedding_text: unsought opportunities, doors of heaven, lucky kshatriyas, embracing battle, challenges as rewards

sanskrit: अथ चेत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं सङ्ग्रामं न करिष्यसि । ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥ ३३ ॥
transliteration: atha cet tvam imaṁ dharmyaṁ saṅgrāmaṁ na kariṣyasi tataḥ sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ ca hitvā pāpam avāpsyasi

meanings.english: If, however, you do not perform your religious duty of fighting, then you will certainly incur sins for neglecting your duties and thus lose your reputation as a fighter.
meanings.hindi: किन्तु यदि तुम युद्ध करने के अपने धर्म का पालन नहीं करोगे, तो अपने कर्तव्य की उपेक्षा करने के कारण पाप के भागी बनोगे और अपनी ख्याति खो दोगे।
meanings.hinglish: Agar tumne apni duty nahi nibhayi, toh tum apna naam bhi kharab karoge aur tumhe pap bhi lagega.
meanings.marathi: परंतु जर तू हे धर्मयुद्ध करणार नाहीस, तर स्वतःचा धर्म आणि कीर्ती सोडून तू पापाचा धनी होशील.

core_meaning: Abandoning duty due to fear or confusion leads to loss of character and negative consequences.
use_cases: preventing the neglect of core responsibilities
realizing that avoiding duty doesn't bring peace, only infamy
motivating oneself to finish what was started
embedding_text: neglecting duty, incurring sin, losing reputation, consequences of inaction, svadharma abandoned

sanskrit: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्ग जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् । तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥ ३७ ॥
transliteration: hato vā prāpsyasi svargaṁ jitvā vā bhokṣyase mahīm tasmād uttiṣṭha kaunteya yuddhāya kṛta-niścayaḥ

meanings.english: O son of Kunti, either you will be killed on the battlefield and attain the heavenly planets, or you will conquer and enjoy the earthly kingdom. Therefore, get up with determination and fight.
meanings.hindi: हे कुन्तीपुत्र! यदि तुम मारे गए तो स्वर्ग प्राप्त करोगे और यदि जीत गए तो पृथ्वी के राज्य का भोग करोगे। अतः दृढ़ निश्चय करके युद्ध के लिए खड़े हो जाओ।
meanings.hinglish: Agar mare gaye toh swarg milega, aur agar jeet gaye toh dharti ka raj paoge. Isliye utho aur pakke irade ke saath laddo.
meanings.marathi: हे कुंतीपुत्रा, तू मारला गेलास तर स्वर्ग मिळवशील आणि जिंकलास तर पृथ्वीचे राज्य उपभोगशील. म्हणून युद्धाचा निश्चय करून उभा राहा.

core_meaning: A 'win-win' philosophy for someone aligned with their duty; failure is not a loss if the effort is righteous.
use_cases: ultimate motivational verse for any high-stakes task
encouraging someone to take a calculated risk
building mental toughness in competitive environments
focusing on action rather than fear of results
embedding_text: get up and fight, win-win situation, determination to act, uttistha kaunteya, heavenly planets or earthly kingdom

sanskrit: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ । ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥ ३८ ॥
transliteration: sukha-duḥkhe same kṛtvā lābhālābhau jayājayau tataḥ yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṁ pāpam avāpsyasi

meanings.english: Do thou fight for the sake of fighting, without considering happiness or distress, loss or gain, victory or defeat – and by so doing you shall never incur sin.
meanings.hindi: सुख-दुख, लाभ-हानि और जय-पराजय को समान समझकर तुम केवल युद्ध के लिए युद्ध करो; ऐसा करने से तुम्हें पाप नहीं लगेगा।
meanings.hinglish: Khushi-gam, fayda-nuksan aur haar-jeet ki chinta chhodo. Bas apna kaam karo, tabhi tum bandhan se mukt rahoge.
meanings.marathi: सुख-दुःख, लाभ-हानी आणि जय-पराजय समान मानून तू युद्धासाठी तयार हो, म्हणजे तुला पाप लागणार नाही.

core_meaning: Equanimity (Samatvam): Performing duty with mental balance regarding the outcomes.
use_cases: improving focus by ignoring external praise or criticism
dealing with the ups and downs of life/business
finding peace in the process rather than the goal
embedding_text: equanimity in happiness and distress, fight for the sake of fighting, neutral to results, avoiding sin through detachment

sanskrit: नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते । स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥ ४० ॥
transliteration: nehābhikrama-nāśo ’sti pratyavāyaḥ na vidyate sv-alpam apy asya dharmasya trāyate mahato bhayāt

meanings.english: In this endeavor there is no loss or diminution, and a little advancement on this path can protect one from the most dangerous type of fear.
meanings.hindi: इस मार्ग पर किया गया थोड़ा सा भी प्रयास कभी व्यर्थ नहीं जाता, और इस धर्म का अल्प अभ्यास भी बड़े से बड़े भय से रक्षा करता है।
meanings.hinglish: Is raste par ki gayi thodi si bhi mehnat kabhi bekar nahi jati, aur thoda sa bhi badlav tumhe bade darr se bacha sakta hai.
meanings.marathi: या मार्गावर केलेल्या सुरुवातीचा नाश होत नाही आणि कोणताही दोष लागत नाही. या धर्माचे थोडेसेही आचरण मोठ्या भयापासून रक्षण करते.

core_meaning: The permanent value of spiritual or selfless effort; no effort is ever wasted.
use_cases: encouragement for beginners in any discipline
realizing that positive habits have compounding permanent effects
finding value in incomplete but sincere efforts
reducing anxiety about the long-term future
embedding_text: no loss in endeavor, protection from fear, small progress matters, permanent results of spiritual work, nehabhikrama nasho sti

sanskrit: व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरूनन्दन । बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्ययसायिनाम् ॥ ४१ ॥
transliteration: vyavasāyātmikā buddhir ekeha kuru-nandana bahu-śākhā hy anantāś ca buddhayo ’vyavasāyinām

meanings.english: Those who are on this path are resolute in purpose, and their aim is one. O beloved child of the Kurus, the intelligence of those who are irresolute is many-branched.
meanings.hindi: हे कुरुनन्दन! इस मार्ग पर चलने वालों का लक्ष्य एक ही होता है और उनकी बुद्धि स्थिर होती है। किन्तु जो स्थिर बुद्धि नहीं हैं, उनकी बुद्धि अनेक शाखाओं में बँटी रहती है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo log focused hote hain unka goal ek hota hai. Par jo confused rehte hain, unka dimaag hazar alag-alag baaton me bhatakta rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: हे कुरुनंदना, या मार्गावर असणाऱ्यांची बुद्धी निश्चयात्मक आणि एकच असते; परंतु धरसोड वृत्तीच्या लोकांची बुद्धी अनेक शाखांनी युक्त आणि अनंत असते.

core_meaning: Success and peace come from single-pointed focus (Ekagrata); a scattered mind leads to endless confusion.
use_cases: combating multi-tasking and lack of concentration
defining a single life mission or goal
building strong willpower for a specific project
embedding_text: resolute purpose, one aim, many-branched intelligence, scattered mind vs focused mind, vyavasayatmika buddhi

sanskrit: भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् । व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥ ४४ ॥
transliteration: bhogaiśvarya-prasaktānāṁ tayāpahṛta-cetasām vyavasāyātmikā buddhiḥ samādhau na vidhīyate

meanings.english: In the minds of those who are too attached to sense enjoyment and material opulence, and who are bewildered by such things, the resolute determination for devotional service to the Supreme Lord does not take place.
meanings.hindi: जो लोग भोग-विलास और ऐश्वर्य में बहुत अधिक आसक्त हैं और ऐसी चीजों से भ्रमित हैं, उनके मन में ईश्वर के प्रति दृढ़ निश्चय उत्पन्न नहीं होता।
meanings.hinglish: Jo log hamesha maze aur paise ke peeche bhagte hain, unka mann kabhi bhi stable nahi ho sakta aur na hi wo bhagwan se jud paate hain.
meanings.marathi: जे लोक उपभोग आणि ऐश्वर्य यामध्ये अतिशय आसक्त आहेत आणि ज्यांचे चित्त या गोष्टींनी हरण केले आहे, त्यांच्या ठायी निश्चयात्मक बुद्धी समाधीमध्ये स्थिर होत नाही.

core_meaning: Excessive attachment to luxury and sensory pleasure prevents deep focus and spiritual growth.
use_cases: identifying the root cause of mental restlessness
understanding why discipline is hard in a consumerist world
balancing material comfort with mental stability
breaking free from addiction to sensory 'cheap thrills'
realizing the barrier to deep meditation
embedding_text: attachment to enjoyment, material opulence, bewildered mind, lack of determination, samadhi impossible for sense-seekers

sanskrit: त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन । निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥ ४५ ॥
transliteration: trai-guṇya-viṣayā vedā nistrai-guṇyo bhavārjuna nirdvandvo nitya-sattva-stho niryoga-kṣema ātmavān

meanings.english: The Vedas deal mainly with the subject of the three modes of material nature. O Arjuna, become transcendental to these three modes. Be free from all dualities and from all anxieties for gain and safety, and be established in the self.
meanings.hindi: वेदों में मुख्य रूप से प्रकृति के तीन गुणों का वर्णन है। हे अर्जुन! तुम इन तीनों गुणों से ऊपर उठो। समस्त द्वंद्वों से मुक्त होकर, लाभ और सुरक्षा की चिंता छोड़ आत्म-स्थित बनो।
meanings.hinglish: Duniya ke teen gunon (modes) se upar utho. Profit aur loss ki tension chhodo aur bas apne 'Self' me tike raho.
meanings.marathi: हे अर्जुना, वेद प्रकृतीच्या तीन गुणांशी संबंधित आहेत, तू या तीन गुणांच्या पलीकडे जा. सुख-दुःखादी द्वंद्वापासून मुक्त हो, नित्य सत्त्वगुणात राहा आणि योगक्षेमाची काळजी सोडून आत्मनिष्ठ हो.

core_meaning: Rise above the 'dualism' of life (win/loss, joy/sorrow) and stop worrying about security; focus on your true nature.
use_cases: reducing anxiety about financial or physical security
finding mental balance during emotional swings
moving beyond 'good vs bad' binary thinking
achieving a state of flow that isn't dependent on circumstances
embedding_text: transcend three modes, free from dualities, niryoga kshema, established in the self, beyond anxiety for gain and safety

sanskrit: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन । मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥ ४७ ॥
transliteration: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo ’stv akarmaṇi

meanings.english: You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.
meanings.hindi: तुम्हें केवल अपना कर्तव्य करने का अधिकार है, किन्तु उसके परिणामों पर तुम्हारा कोई अधिकार नहीं है। स्वयं को कर्मों के फल का कारण मत मानो, और न ही अकर्मण्यता (काम न करने) के प्रति आसक्त होओ।
meanings.hinglish: Tera kaam bas mehnat karna hai, result tere hath me nahi hai. Result ki chinta mat kar, par kaam chori bhi mat kar.
meanings.marathi: तुला केवळ तुझे कर्तव्य करण्याचा अधिकार आहे, फळावर कधीही नाही. तू कर्माच्या फळाचा हेतू होऊ नकोस आणि कर्म न करण्याकडे तुझी आसक्ती नसावी.

core_meaning: The golden rule of Karma Yoga: Focus entirely on the process and effort; detach from the outcome to remain free from stress.
use_cases: the most famous verse for work-life balance and stress management
preventing burnout by releasing the obsession with results
shifting focus from 'what will I get' to 'what can I do'
embedding_text: right to work but not to results, karmanye vadhikaraste, detachment from fruits of action, cause of action, avoiding inaction

sanskrit: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय । सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥ ४८ ॥
transliteration: yoga-sthaḥ kuru karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā dhanañ-jaya siddhy-asiddhyoḥ samo bhūtvā samatvaṁ yoga ucyate

meanings.english: Perform your duty equipoised, O Arjuna, abandoning all attachment to success or failure. Such equanimity is called yoga.
meanings.hindi: हे धनञ्जय! जय-पराजय और सफलता-विफलता की आसक्ति को त्यागकर, समभाव में स्थिर होकर अपना कर्तव्य करो। ऐसी समता ही योग कहलाती है।
meanings.hinglish: Success aur failure ki tension chhod kar, mann ko balance me rakh kar apna kaam karo. Is balance ko hi Yoga kehte hain.
meanings.marathi: हे धनंजया, तू आसक्ती सोडून आणि सिद्धी व असिद्धीमध्ये समान राहून योगात स्थिर होऊन कर्मे कर. समत्व भाव यालाच योग म्हणतात.

core_meaning: Yoga is defined as 'Samatvam' (equanimity)—maintaining a steady mind regardless of external success or failure.
use_cases: staying calm during market crashes or project failures
embedding_text: equipoised in success and failure, samatvam yoga ucyate, abandoning attachment, yoga means equanimity, performing duty in yoga

sanskrit: दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय । बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥ ४९ ॥
transliteration: dūreṇa hy avaraṁ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañ-jaya buddhau śaraṇam anviccha kṛpaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ

meanings.english: O Dhananjaya, keep all abominable activities far distant by devotional service, and in that consciousness surrender unto the Lord. Those who want to enjoy the fruits of their work are misers.
meanings.hindi: हे धनञ्जय! बुद्धियोग (कृष्णभावनामृत) के द्वारा तुच्छ कर्मों से दूर रहो और उसी चेतना में शरण लो। जो लोग अपने कर्मों का फल भोगना चाहते हैं, वे कृपण (कंजूस) हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Apne dimaag ko sahi jagah lagao aur lalach waale kaamon se door raho. Jo sirf result ke liye kaam karte hain, wo dunya ke sabse gareeb log hain.
meanings.marathi: हे धनंजया, बुद्धियोगाच्या (समत्वबुद्धीच्या) तुलनेत सकाम कर्म अत्यंत हलक्या दर्जाचे आहे. म्हणून तू बुद्धीचा आश्रय घे, कारण फळाची इच्छा धरणारे लोक दीन (कृपण) असतात.

core_meaning: Action motivated by personal greed/reward is inferior; acting with higher intelligence and surrender is superior.
use_cases: surrendering ego to a higher cause
choosing quality of consciousness over quantity of results
embedding_text: buddhi yoga, fruitive results are for misers, abominable activities, take shelter in intelligence, higher consciousness

sanskrit: बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते । तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥ ५० ॥
transliteration: buddhi-yukto jahātīha ubhe sukṛta-duṣkṛte tasmād yogāya yujyasva yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam

meanings.english: A man engaged in devotional service rids himself of both good and bad reactions even in this life. Therefore strive for yoga, which is the art of all work.
meanings.hindi: भक्ति में लगा हुआ व्यक्ति इसी जीवन में अच्छे और बुरे दोनों कर्मों के फलों से मुक्त हो जाता है। अतः योग के लिए प्रयत्न करो, जो समस्त कर्मों में कुशलता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan smartness aur devotion se kaam karta hai, wo achhe-bure dono results ki tension se upar uth jata hai. Isliye 'Yoga' sikho, kyunki kaam karne ki asli art yahi hai.
meanings.marathi: बुद्धियुक्त मनुष्य याच जन्मात पुण्य आणि पाप या दोन्हींचा त्याग करतो. म्हणून तू योगाचा अवलंब कर, कारण कर्मामधील कुशलता म्हणजेच योग होय.

core_meaning: Yoga is defined as 'Skill in Action' (Karmasu Kaushalam)—acting with such wisdom that you remain untouched by reactions.
use_cases: mastering professional skills through deep focus
learning to work efficiently without emotional exhaustion
achieving a 'flow state' in daily tasks
understanding that high-quality work is a form of spiritual practice
resolving the guilt of past mistakes by focusing on present 'skillful' action
embedding_text: yoga is skill in action, karmasu kaushalam, rid of good and bad reactions, art of all work, devotional service in action

sanskrit: स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव । स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥ ५४ ॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca sthita-prajñasya kā bhāṣā samādhi-sthasya keśava sthita-dhīḥ kiṁ prabhāṣeta kim āsīta vrajeta kim

meanings.english: Arjuna said: O Krishna, what are the symptoms of one whose consciousness is thus merged in transcendence? How does he speak, and what is his language? How does he sit, and how does he walk?
meanings.hindi: अर्जुन ने कहा: हे कृष्ण! जिसकी बुद्धि स्थिर है और जो समाधि में स्थित है, उसके क्या लक्षण हैं? वह कैसे बोलता है, कैसे बैठता है और कैसे चलता है?
meanings.hinglish: Arjun ne pucha: Krishna, ek steady mind wale insaan ki pehchan kya hai? Wo kaise baat karta hai, kaise uthta-baithta hai aur kaise chalta hai?
meanings.marathi: अर्जुन म्हणाला: हे केशवा, स्थिरबुद्धी असलेल्या आणि समाधिस्थ असलेल्या मनुष्याचे लक्षण काय? तो स्थिरबुद्धी मनुष्य कसा बोलतो, कसा बसतो आणि कसा चालतो?

core_meaning: The inquiry into the practical behavior and characteristics of a person with a perfectly steady mind (Sthitaprajna).
use_cases: seeking a role model for mental stability
understanding how spiritual values manifest in daily behavior
evaluating one's own progress in character building
learning that spirituality is practical, not just theoretical
analyzing the connection between internal state and external conduct
embedding_text: symptoms of a steady mind, sthitaprajna qualities, how does he walk and talk, inquiry into transcendence, samadhi signs

sanskrit: दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः । वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते ॥ ५६ ॥
transliteration: duḥkheṣv anudvigna-manāḥ sukheṣu vigata-spṛhaḥ vīta-rāga-bhaya-krodhaḥ sthita-dhīr munir ucyate

meanings.english: One who is not disturbed in mind even amidst the threefold miseries or elated when there is happiness, and who is free from attachment, fear and anger, is called a sage of steady mind.
meanings.hindi: जो दुखों के आने पर विचलित नहीं होता, सुखों की प्राप्ति की इच्छा नहीं करता, और जो आसक्ति, भय तथा क्रोध से मुक्त है, वही स्थिर बुद्धि वाला मुनि कहलाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Dukh me jo pareshan na ho, sukh me jo phule na samaye, aur jise na kisi se lagav ho, na darr, na gussa—wahi asli steady mind wala hai.
meanings.marathi: दुःखात ज्याचे मन उद्विग्न होत नाही, सुखाची ज्याला ओढ नाही आणि ज्याचे राग, भय व क्रोध निघून गेले आहेत, असा मनुष्य स्थिरबुद्धी मुनी म्हटला जातो.

core_meaning: Emotional Intelligence (EQ): Mastery over reactions to external circumstances like pain, pleasure, fear, and anger.
use_cases: managing anger and fear in high-pressure situations
avoiding 'hedonic adaptation' or chasing temporary highs
cultivating a neutral and peaceful temperament
staying grounded during periods of intense success or failure
embedding_text: undisturbed in misery, not elated in happiness, free from attachment fear and anger, steady mind, sthita-dhir munir

sanskrit: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥ ५८ ॥
transliteration: yadā saṁharate cāyaṁ kūrmo ’ṅgānīva sarvaśaḥ indriyāṇīndriyārthebhyas tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā

meanings.english: One who is able to withdraw his senses from sense objects, as the tortoise draws its limbs within the shell, is firmly fixed in perfect consciousness.
meanings.hindi: जिस प्रकार कछुआ अपने अंगों को समेटकर खोल के भीतर कर लेता है, उसी प्रकार जो अपनी इन्द्रियों को इन्द्रिय-विषयों से खींच लेता है, उसकी बुद्धि स्थिर होती है।
meanings.hinglish: Jaise kachua khatra dekh kar apne haath-pair andar kar leta hai, waise hi jo apni senses ko distractions se hata sake, wahi real sense me intelligent hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याप्रमाणे कासव आपले सर्व अवयव आपल्या पाठीच्या कवचात ओढून घेते, त्याप्रमाणे जो आपली इंद्रिये इंद्रियविषयांतून काढून घेतो, त्याची प्रज्ञा स्थिर झाली असे समजावे.

core_meaning: Self-control and sensory withdrawal (Pratyahara): The ability to shut out distractions at will.
use_cases: avoiding digital distractions (social media, etc.) while working
practicing discipline in eating or spending habits
protecting one's mind from toxic environments or information
learning the power of 'saying no' to sensory urges
embedding_text: tortoise withdrawing limbs, control of senses, withdrawing from sense objects, fixed consciousness, sensory discipline

sanskrit: विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः । रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥ ५९ ॥
transliteration: viṣayā vinivartante nirāhārasya dehinaḥ rasa-varjaṁ raso ’py asya paraṁ dṛṣṭvā nivartate

meanings.english: Though the embodied soul may be restricted from sense enjoyment, the taste for sense objects remains. But, ceasing such engagements by experiencing a higher taste, he is fixed in consciousness.
meanings.hindi: देहधारी जीव इन्द्रियभोग से भले ही रुक जाए, पर उसमें इन्द्रिय-विषयों की लालसा बनी रहती है। लेकिन उच्च रस (परम तत्व) का अनुभव करने पर उसकी यह लालसा भी समाप्त हो जाती है।
meanings.hinglish: Sirf bahar se rokne se lalach khatam nahi hoti. Jab tumhe koi better cheez (higher taste) mil jati hai, tabhi tum purani buri aadaton ko hamesha ke liye chhod paate ho.
meanings.marathi: इंद्रियांना विषयांचा आहार न देणाऱ्या मनुष्याचे विषय तर निवृत्त होतात, पण त्यांची गोडी (चव) मनात उरतेच. परंतु श्रेष्ठ तत्त्वाचा अनुभव आल्यावर या विषयांची गोडीही नाहीशी होते.

core_meaning: Positive Replacement: Real discipline isn't about suppression, but finding a 'higher taste' (a better purpose or joy) that makes lower habits lose their appeal.
use_cases: breaking addictions by finding a more rewarding hobby or purpose
replacing junk food with the 'higher taste' of health and energy
shifting from seeking validation to seeking self-growth
understanding why 'willpower' alone often fails without a higher goal
finding deep joy in spiritual practice that outweighs worldly pleasures
embedding_text: higher taste, ceasing sense enjoyment, taste for sense objects remains, param dristva nivartate, spiritual satisfaction

sanskrit: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः । इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥ ६० ॥
transliteration: yatato hy api kaunteya puruṣasya vipaścitaḥ indriyāṇi pramāthīni haranti prasabhaṁ manaḥ

meanings.english: The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them.
meanings.hindi: हे कुन्तीपुत्र! इन्द्रियाँ इतनी बलवान और मथ डालने वाली हैं कि वे संयम का प्रयत्न करने वाले बुद्धिमान मनुष्य के मन को भी बलपूर्वक हर लेती हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Indriyaan (senses) itni powerful hoti hain Arjun, ki wo ek samajhdaar insaan ke mann ko bhi zabardasti bhatka sakti hain, chahe wo unhe control karne ki kitni bhi koshish kare.
meanings.marathi: हे कौंतेया, इंद्रिये इतकी प्रबळ आणि खळबळ माजवणारी आहेत की, ती संयम राखण्याचा प्रयत्न करणाऱ्या विवेकी मनुष्याचे मनही बळजबरीने हिरावून घेतात.

core_meaning: The immense power of the senses: Even with knowledge, willpower alone is often insufficient to resist strong sensory cravings.
use_cases: understanding the root of 'relapse' in bad habits
realizing that knowledge is not the same as self-control
staying humble regarding one's own self-discipline
identifying why focus is difficult even for the 'intelligent'
embedding_text: senses carry away the mind, strong and impetuous senses, man of discrimination, difficulty in sense control, sensory agitation

sanskrit: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते । सङ्गात्सञ्जायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥ ६२ ॥
transliteration: dhyāyato viṣayān puṁsaḥ saṅgas teṣūpajāyate saṅgāt sañjāyate kāmaḥ kāmāt krodho ’bhijāyate

meanings.english: While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them, and from such attachment lust develops, and from lust anger arises.
meanings.hindi: इन्द्रिय-विषयों का चिन्तन करने से मनुष्य की उनमें आसक्ति पैदा होती है, आसक्ति से कामना (इच्छा) जन्म लेती है, और कामना पूरी न होने पर क्रोध उत्पन्न होता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jab tum kisi cheez ke baare me baar-baar sochte ho, toh usse lagav (attachment) ho jata hai. Attachment se lalach badhti hai, aur lalach poori na ho toh gussa aata hai.
meanings.marathi: विषयांचे चिंतन करणाऱ्या मनुष्याची त्या विषयांच्या ठिकाणी आसक्ती निर्माण होते, आसक्तीतून इच्छा (काम) निर्माण होते आणि इच्छेत अडथळा आला की क्रोध निर्माण होतो.

core_meaning: The Ladder of Fall (Part 1): Thinking about a desire is the seed of attachment, which leads to obsession and eventually anger.
use_cases: tracing the psychology of how an obsession begins
understanding the relationship between unfulfilled desires and anger
identifying 'mental screen time' as a precursor to addiction
learning to nip destructive thoughts in the bud
analyzing the cause of frustration in daily life
embedding_text: contemplating sense objects, attachment develops, lust from attachment, anger from lust, psychology of desire

sanskrit: क्रोधाद्भ‍वति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः । स्मृतिभ्रंशाद्बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥ ६३ ॥
transliteration: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ smṛti-bhraṁśād buddhi-nāśo buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati

meanings.english: From anger, complete delusion arises, and from delusion bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost, and when intelligence is lost one falls down again into the material pool.
meanings.hindi: क्रोध से सम्मोह (मूढ़ भाव) उत्पन्न होता है, सम्मोह से स्मृति भ्रमित हो जाती है। जब स्मृति भ्रमित होती है, तो बुद्धि (विवेक) नष्ट हो जाती है, और बुद्धि नष्ट होने पर मनुष्य का पतन हो जाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Gusse se dimaag kaam karna band kar deta hai (confusion). Confusion se insaan purani seekh bhool jata hai. Jab akal chali jati hai, toh insaan barbad ho jata hai.
meanings.marathi: क्रोधातून मोह उत्पन्न होतो, मोहातून स्मृती भ्रमिष्ट होते, स्मृतीभ्रंशातून बुद्धीचा नाश होतो आणि बुद्धीचा नाश झाला की मनुष्याचा पूर्ण अध:पात होतो.

core_meaning: The Ladder of Fall (Part 2): Anger destroys logic and memory, leading to total failure of judgment and character.
use_cases: understanding the scientific/psychological impact of 'blind rage'
preventing life-altering mistakes made in a split second of anger
recognizing the importance of preserving 'Smriti' (values and lessons) during crisis
learning why angry people can't see the truth
motivating oneself to practice anger management
embedding_text: anger leads to delusion, loss of memory, destruction of intelligence, downfall of man, consequences of anger

sanskrit: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना । न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥ ६६ ॥
transliteration: nāsti buddhir ayuktasya na cāyuktasya bhāvanā na cābhāvayataḥ śāntir aśāntasya kutaḥ sukham

meanings.english: One who is not connected with the Supreme can have neither transcendental intelligence nor a steady mind, without which there is no possibility of peace. And how can there be any happiness without peace?
meanings.hindi: जो योगयुक्त नहीं है, उसके पास न तो दिव्य बुद्धि है और न ही स्थिर मन। जिसके पास शांति नहीं, उसे सुख कैसे मिल सकता है?
meanings.hinglish: Jiska mann balance me nahi hai, uske paas na akal hai na stability. Bina shanti ke khushi kabhi nahi mil sakti.
meanings.marathi: ज्याचे मन आवरलेले नाही, त्याला श्रेष्ठ बुद्धी लाभत नाही आणि अशा मनुष्याला एकाग्रताही लाभत नाही. ज्याला शांती नाही, त्याला सुख कोठून मिळणार?

core_meaning: The Hierarchy of Wellbeing: Focus leads to Peace; Peace leads to Happiness. Without a disciplined mind, happiness is impossible.
use_cases: explaining why wealth or success doesn't guarantee happiness
prioritizing mental peace over temporary excitement
building the foundation for a happy life through meditation/focus
embedding_text: no peace without steady mind, no happiness without peace, lack of intelligence in undisciplined, necessity of connection with Supreme

sanskrit: या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी । यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥ ६९ ॥
transliteration: yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti saṁyamī yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyataḥ muneḥ

meanings.english: What is night for all beings is the time of awakening for the self-controlled; and the time of awakening for all beings is night for the introspective sage.
meanings.hindi: जो समस्त जीवों के लिए रात्रि है, वह संयमी (योगी) के जागने का समय है; और जिसमें समस्त जीव जागते हैं, वह आत्मज्ञानी मुनि के लिए रात्रि है।
meanings.hinglish: Dunya jis cheez ke peeche bhagti hai (paisa, fame), ek samajhdaar insaan ke liye wo bekar hai. Aur jis cheez ko dunya ignore karti hai (self-growth, peace), samajhdaar insaan usi me laga rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: सर्व प्राण्यांची जी रात्र असते, त्या वेळी संयमी मनुष्य जागा असतो आणि ज्या वेळी सर्व प्राणी जागे असतात, ती ज्ञानी मुनीसाठी रात्र असते.

core_meaning: Difference in Perspective: A wise person values what the world ignores (inner peace) and ignores what the world values (material obsession).
use_cases: developing a unique 'contrarian' mindset for success
staying undisturbed by social peer pressure
finding value in solitude and introspection when others are partying/distracted
choosing long-term meaning over short-term gratification
embedding_text: night for all beings is awakening for sage, different perspectives of wise vs worldly, self-control and introspection

sanskrit: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् । तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे स शान्तिमाप्‍नोति न कामकामी ॥ ७० ॥
transliteration: āpūryamāṇam acala-pratiṣṭhaṁ samudram āpaḥ praviśanti yadvat tadvat kāmā yaṁ praviśanti sarve sa śāntim āpnoti na kāma-kāmī

meanings.english: A person who is not disturbed by the incessant flow of desires – that enter like rivers into the ocean, which is ever being filled but is always still – can alone achieve peace, and not the man who strives to satisfy such desires.
meanings.hindi: जैसे अनेक नदियाँ समुद्र में गिरती हैं पर समुद्र विचलित नहीं होता, वैसे ही जिसके मन में इच्छाएँ आती तो हैं पर उसे अशांत नहीं करतीं, वही शांति पाता है। जो इच्छाओं के पीछे भागता है, उसे शांति नहीं मिलती।
meanings.hinglish: Jaise kitni bhi nadiyaan samundar me mil jayein, samundar kabhi nahi ubalta, waise hi jiske mann me hazaar khwahishein aayein par wo shant rahe, wahi asli peace me hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याप्रमाणे चारी बाजूंनी भरून वाहणाऱ्या नद्या समुद्रात प्रवेश करतात, तरीही समुद्र शांतच राहतो, त्याचप्रमाणे ज्याच्या ठायी सर्व इच्छा विचलित न करता समाविष्ट होतात, तोच शांती प्राप्त करतो; इच्छांच्या मागे धावणारा नाही.

core_meaning: The Ocean Mindset: Be so full and deep within that external desires enter you but do not disturb your inner stillness.
use_cases: maintaining calm in a world of endless marketing and temptations
developing inner fulfillment so that external wants don't cause misery
practicing 'detachment' while still living in the world
visualizing a calm and deep mental state during meditation
learning the difference between having a desire and being 'driven' by it
embedding_text: ocean remains still despite rivers entering, peace for the undisturbed, not for the desire-seeker, fullness within, ocean mindset

sanskrit: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः । निर्ममो निरहङ्कार स शान्तिमधिगच्छति ॥ ७१ ॥
transliteration: vihāya kāmān yaḥ sarvān pumāṁś carati niḥspṛhaḥ nirmamo nirahaṅkāraḥ sa śāntim adhigacchati

meanings.english: A person who has given up all desires for sense gratification, who lives free from desires, who has given up all sense of proprietorship and is devoid of false ego – he alone can attain real peace.
meanings.hindi: जो मनुष्य समस्त भौतिक इच्छाओं का परित्याग कर देता है, जो लालसा रहित रहता है और जिसने अपनेपन तथा अहंकार का त्याग कर दिया है, वही वास्तविक शांति प्राप्त कर सकता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan apni saari lalach chhod deta hai, jise kisi cheez ka moh-maya nahi rehta aur jo 'main' aur 'mera' wale ego se upar uth jata hai, wahi asli shanti paata hai.
meanings.marathi: जो मनुष्य सर्व वासनांचा त्याग करून स्पृहारहित होऊन वावरतो आणि ज्याचा 'मी' व 'माझे' हा भाव नष्ट झाला आहे, तोच खरी शांती प्राप्त करतो.

core_meaning: The formula for Absolute Peace: Abandoning 'Desire' (greed), 'Proprietorship' (possessiveness), and 'Ego' (false identity).
use_cases: attaining deep mental peace after a major life crisis
letting go of the stress of ownership and property disputes
shifting from 'I want' to 'How can I serve'
reaching a state of zero anxiety and full presence
embedding_text: giving up desires, free from proprietorship, devoid of false ego, attaining real peace, nirmamo nirahankara

sanskrit: एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिःपार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति । स्थित्वास्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥ ७२ ॥
transliteration: eṣā brāhmī sthitiḥ pārtha naināṁ prāpya vimuhyati sthitvāsyām anta-kāle ’pi brahma-nirvāṇam ṛcchati

meanings.english: That is the way of the spiritual and godly life, after attaining which a man is not bewildered. If one is thus situated even at the hour of death, one can enter into the kingdom of God.
meanings.hindi: यह आध्यात्मिक जीवन का मार्ग है, जिसे प्राप्त करके मनुष्य कभी मोहित नहीं होता। यदि कोई मृत्यु के समय भी इस स्थिति में रहता है, तो वह ब्रह्म-निर्वाण (भगवद्धाम) को प्राप्त होता है।
meanings.hinglish: Yahi asli spiritual life hai, ise paane ke baad koi confusion nahi rehti. Agar koi marte waqt bhi is state me rahe, toh use moksha mil jata hai.
meanings.marathi: हे अर्जुना, हीच ब्राह्मी स्थिती (ईश्वरी स्थिती) आहे. ही प्राप्त झाल्यावर मनुष्य कधीही गोंधळून जात नाही. अंताकाळी सुद्धा या स्थितीत राहिल्यास त्याला ब्रह्मनिर्वाण (मोक्ष) प्राप्त होते.

core_meaning: The ultimate goal: A state of consciousness (Brahmi Sthiti) that ends all confusion and leads to liberation, regardless of when it is achieved.
use_cases: finding ultimate clarity and purpose in life
overcoming the fear of death by focusing on consciousness
realizing that it is never too late to start a spiritual journey
sustaining a state of 'unshakeable' mental health
understanding the concept of Nirvana or liberation while alive
embedding_text: brahmi sthiti, spiritual state, not bewildered, end of life attainment, brahma-nirvana, kingdom of God

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन । तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca jyāyasī cet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana tat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśava

meanings.english: Arjuna asks Krishna: If knowledge is superior to action, then why are you asking me to engage in such terrible action?
meanings.hindi: अर्जुन कहते हैं: यदि ज्ञान कर्म से श्रेष्ठ है, तो हे जनार्दन, आप मुझे इस भयंकर कर्म में क्यों लगाते हैं?
meanings.hinglish: Arjun pooch raha hai: agar knowledge action se better hai, toh mujhe itne tough action me kyun daal rahe ho?
meanings.marathi: अर्जुन म्हणतो: जर ज्ञान कर्मापेक्षा श्रेष्ठ असेल, तर मला या कठीण कर्मात का गुंतवत आहात?

core_meaning: Confusion between knowledge and action, and uncertainty about the right path to follow
use_cases: feeling stuck due to too many options
embedding_text: confusion between action and knowledge, overthinking vs taking action, doubt about what is the right path, should I act or wait, feeling stuck in decisions, life direction confusion

sanskrit: व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे । तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्‍नुयाम् ॥
transliteration: vyāmiśreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me tad ekaṁ vada niścitya yena śreyo ’ham āpnuyām

meanings.english: Arjuna says: Your words seem confusing and are bewildering my understanding. Please tell me clearly the one path that will lead me to my highest good.
meanings.hindi: अर्जुन कहते हैं: आपके मिले-जुले वचन मेरी बुद्धि को भ्रमित कर रहे हैं। कृपया स्पष्ट रूप से बताइए कि कौन सा एक मार्ग मेरे लिए सर्वोत्तम होगा।
meanings.hinglish: Arjun bol raha hai: aapki baatein mujhe confuse kar rahi hain. Please clearly batao kaunsa ek path best hai mere liye.
meanings.marathi: अर्जुन म्हणतो: तुमचे मिश्रित बोलणे माझी बुद्धी गोंधळात टाकत आहे. कृपया स्पष्टपणे सांगा की माझ्यासाठी सर्वोत्तम मार्ग कोणता आहे.

core_meaning: Seeking clarity when guidance feels confusing and wanting a clear single direction
use_cases: feeling confused by too much advice
embedding_text: confusion due to mixed advice, too many opinions causing doubt, need clear direction in life, overwhelmed by choices, seeking clarity in decisions, guidance confusion

sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ । ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca loke ’smin dvi-vidhā niṣṭhā purā proktā mayānagha jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām

meanings.english: The Supreme Lord said: In this world, there are two kinds of paths—one is the path of knowledge for the contemplative, and the other is the path of action for those who are active.
meanings.hindi: भगवान कहते हैं: इस संसार में दो प्रकार की निष्ठाएँ हैं—एक ज्ञानयोग (चिंतन करने वालों के लिए) और दूसरी कर्मयोग (कर्म करने वालों के लिए)।
meanings.hinglish: Bhagwan kehte hain: is duniya me do path hain—ek knowledge ka aur ek action ka. Dono alag logon ke liye suitable hote hain.
meanings.marathi: भगवान म्हणतात: या जगात दोन मार्ग आहेत—एक ज्ञानयोग आणि दुसरा कर्मयोग, जे वेगवेगळ्या स्वभावाच्या लोकांसाठी योग्य आहेत.

core_meaning: There are different paths in life—knowledge and action—and each person must follow what suits their nature
use_cases: finding the right path based on personality
should I study more or start working
understanding that different approaches can be right
embedding_text: different life paths knowledge vs action, choosing right path based on personality, clarity in decision making, understanding different approaches, should I think or act, career path confusion

sanskrit: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्य पुरुषोऽश्नुते । न च सन्न्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
transliteration: na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣkarmyaṁ puruṣo ’śnute na ca sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigacchati

meanings.english: A person does not achieve freedom from action by merely avoiding work, nor does one attain perfection simply by renouncing actions.
meanings.hindi: कोई व्यक्ति केवल कर्म न करने से कर्म से मुक्त नहीं होता, और केवल संन्यास लेने से ही सिद्धि प्राप्त नहीं होती।
meanings.hinglish: Sirf kaam na karne se ya sab kuch chhod dene se success ya peace nahi milti. Action lena zaroori hai.
meanings.marathi: केवळ कर्म टाळल्याने मुक्ती मिळत नाही, आणि फक्त संन्यास घेतल्याने सिद्धी प्राप्त होत नाही.

core_meaning: Avoiding action or escaping responsibilities does not lead to growth or success
use_cases: escaping work instead of facing it
embedding_text: avoiding work does not lead to success, procrastination and laziness issues, escaping responsibilities, thinking quitting solves problems, need to take action in life, growth requires action

sanskrit: न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् । कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥
transliteration: na hi kaścit kṣaṇam api jātu tiṣṭhaty akarma-kṛt kāryate hy avaśaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛti-jair guṇaiḥ

meanings.english: No one can remain even for a moment without performing action. Everyone is compelled to act by the qualities born of nature.
meanings.hindi: कोई भी व्यक्ति एक क्षण भी बिना कर्म किए नहीं रह सकता। सभी लोग प्रकृति के गुणों द्वारा कर्म करने के लिए बाध्य होते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Koi bhi insaan ek second ke liye bhi bina action ke nahi reh sakta. Nature sabko automatically action karne pe majboor karti hai.
meanings.marathi: कोणताही मनुष्य एक क्षणही कर्म न करता राहू शकत नाही. प्रत्येकजण प्रकृतीच्या गुणांमुळे कर्म करण्यास बाध्य असतो.

core_meaning: Action is unavoidable—everyone is naturally driven to act, whether consciously or unconsciously
use_cases: feeling restless or unable to sit idle
understanding why we cannot stop thinking or acting
struggling with overactivity or constant thoughts
realizing action is part of human nature
wanting to control actions but feeling driven
embedding_text: humans cannot stay without action, constant activity of mind and body, feeling restless and always doing something, nature drives action automatically, cannot stop thinking or acting, understanding human behavior and action

sanskrit: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् । इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥
transliteration: karmendriyāṇi saṁyamya ya āste manasā smaran indriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyācāraḥ sa ucyate

meanings.english: One who restrains the organs of action but continues to dwell on sense objects in the mind is deluded and is called a hypocrite.
meanings.hindi: जो व्यक्ति बाहरी इन्द्रियों को तो नियंत्रित करता है, लेकिन मन में इन्द्रिय विषयों का चिंतन करता रहता है, वह भ्रमित है और उसे कपटी कहा जाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan bahar se control dikhata hai par andar se wahi desires sochta rehta hai, woh actually hypocrite hai.
meanings.marathi: जो मनुष्य बाह्य इंद्रियांना नियंत्रणात ठेवतो पण मनात इंद्रिय विषयांचा विचार करत राहतो, तो मूर्ख आणि कपटी समजला जातो.

core_meaning: Outer control without inner discipline is hypocrisy; true control must come from the mind
use_cases: pretending to be disciplined but lacking inner control
saying one thing but thinking another
embedding_text: hypocrisy between inner thoughts and outer actions, lack of true self control, hidden desires despite discipline, inner conflict and self deception, pretending to be disciplined but mind is uncontrolled

sanskrit: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन । कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥
transliteration: yas tv indriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna karmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśiṣyate

meanings.english: But one who controls the senses with the mind and engages in action without attachment is superior, O Arjuna.
meanings.hindi: हे अर्जुन, जो मन द्वारा इन्द्रियों को नियंत्रित कर, बिना आसक्ति के कर्म करता है, वही श्रेष्ठ है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan apne mind se senses ko control karke bina attachment ke kaam karta hai, wahi best hai.
meanings.marathi: जो मनाने इंद्रियांना नियंत्रित करून आसक्तीशिवाय कर्म करतो, तो श्रेष्ठ मानला जातो.

core_meaning: True excellence comes from self-control and performing actions without attachment to results
use_cases: how to stay focused while working
avoiding distraction while doing important tasks
embedding_text: self control and discipline in action, working without attachment to results, staying focused and avoiding distractions, performing duties with calm mind, how to work without stress or overthinking

sanskrit: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः । शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
transliteration: niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hy akarmaṇaḥ śarīra-yātrāpi ca te na prasidhyed akarmaṇaḥ

meanings.english: Perform your prescribed duties, for action is better than inaction. Even maintaining your body would not be possible without action.
meanings.hindi: अपने निर्धारित कर्म करो, क्योंकि कर्म करना अकर्म से श्रेष्ठ है। बिना कर्म किए तो शरीर का पालन भी संभव नहीं है।
meanings.hinglish: Apna duty karo, kyunki kaam karna kuch na karne se better hai. Bina action ke basic life bhi sustain nahi ho sakti.
meanings.marathi: आपले नियत कर्म करा, कारण कर्म हे अकर्मापेक्षा श्रेष्ठ आहे. कर्म न करता शरीरही टिकू शकत नाही.

core_meaning: Fulfilling your duties through action is essential—even basic survival depends on it
use_cases: need discipline to maintain life balance
embedding_text: importance of doing daily duties, action is better than inaction, motivation to work and avoid laziness, responsibility in life, discipline for survival and growth, why action is necessary

sanskrit: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः । तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्गः समाचर ॥
transliteration: yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ tad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samācara

meanings.english: Work done as a sacrifice for a higher purpose frees one from bondage, but work done for selfish reasons binds one. Therefore, perform your duties without attachment.
meanings.hindi: यज्ञ (उच्च उद्देश्य) के लिए किया गया कर्म बंधन से मुक्त करता है, जबकि स्वार्थ के लिए किया गया कर्म बंधन में डालता है। इसलिए, आसक्ति रहित होकर कर्म करो।
meanings.hinglish: Jo kaam higher purpose ke liye kiya jata hai wo free karta hai, aur jo sirf selfish reasons ke liye hota hai wo bandhan ban jata hai. Isliye attachment ke bina kaam karo.
meanings.marathi: उच्च हेतूसाठी केलेले कर्म बंधनातून मुक्त करते, तर स्वार्थासाठी केलेले कर्म बंधन निर्माण करते. म्हणून आसक्तीशिवाय कर्म करा.

core_meaning: Work done with a higher purpose liberates, while selfish action creates bondage
use_cases: doing work only for money vs purpose
feeling stuck due to selfish goals
finding higher purpose in daily actions
embedding_text: working with purpose vs selfish work, finding meaning in work, how to work without attachment, purpose driven life, avoiding selfish actions, doing work for higher cause and fulfillment

sanskrit: सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः । अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥
transliteration: saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛṣṭvā purovāca prajāpatiḥ anena prasaviṣyadhvam eṣa vo ’stv iṣṭa-kāma-dhuk

meanings.english: At the beginning of creation, the Creator said: By performing sacrifice (selfless action), you will prosper, and this will fulfill all your desires.
meanings.hindi: सृष्टि के आरंभ में प्रजापति ने कहा: यज्ञ (निःस्वार्थ कर्म) के द्वारा तुम समृद्धि प्राप्त करोगे और यह तुम्हारी इच्छाओं को पूरा करेगा।
meanings.hinglish: Creation ke start me hi bataya gaya tha ki selfless action (yagya) se hi growth aur success milti hai, aur wahi tumhari wishes fulfill karta hai.
meanings.marathi: सृष्टीच्या प्रारंभी प्रजापती म्हणाले: यज्ञ (निःस्वार्थ कर्म) केल्याने तुम्हाला समृद्धी मिळेल आणि तुमच्या इच्छा पूर्ण होतील.

core_meaning: Selfless action aligned with a higher purpose leads to growth, prosperity, and fulfillment of desires
use_cases: wanting success and growth in life
embedding_text: selfless action leads to success and growth, how to achieve goals through purpose, fulfillment through contribution, importance of giving and higher purpose, prosperity through meaningful work

sanskrit: देवान्भावयतातेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः । परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥
transliteration: devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ parasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śreyaḥ param avāpsyatha

meanings.english: By nourishing the divine forces through selfless action, they will nourish you in return. By supporting each other, you will achieve the highest good.
meanings.hindi: यज्ञ (निःस्वार्थ कर्म) के द्वारा देवताओं का पोषण करो, और वे तुम्हारा पोषण करेंगे। इस प्रकार एक-दूसरे का सहयोग करते हुए तुम सर्वोत्तम कल्याण प्राप्त करोगे।
meanings.hinglish: Agar tum selfless actions se contribute karoge, toh nature aur system bhi tumhe support karega. Mutual support se hi real success milti hai.
meanings.marathi: यज्ञाद्वारे देवतांचे पोषण करा, ते तुम्हाला पोषण करतील. परस्पर सहकार्याने तुम्ही सर्वोच्च कल्याण प्राप्त कराल.

core_meaning: Mutual support and selfless contribution create a cycle of growth and lead to the highest success
embedding_text: mutual support and teamwork leads to success, giving and receiving in life, collaboration and cooperation importance, building relationships for growth, helping others and getting help in return

sanskrit: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः । तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥
transliteration: iṣṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ tair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ

meanings.english: The divine forces, satisfied by selfless actions, will provide you with what you need. But one who enjoys these gifts without sharing or giving back is considered a thief.
meanings.hindi: यज्ञ (निःस्वार्थ कर्म) से प्रसन्न देवता तुम्हें इच्छित भोग देंगे। लेकिन जो व्यक्ति बिना लौटाए या साझा किए उनका उपभोग करता है, वह चोर कहलाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Selfless action se nature tumhe rewards deti hai, lekin agar tum sirf lete ho aur kuch return nahi karte, toh wo selfish aur galat hai.
meanings.marathi: यज्ञामुळे देवता तुम्हाला इच्छित फळ देतात. पण जे मिळाले ते परत न देता फक्त स्वतःसाठी वापरणारा माणूस चोर समजला जातो.

core_meaning: Taking without giving back is selfish; true balance comes from contribution and sharing
use_cases: importance of giving back to society
receiving benefits without effort or contribution
embedding_text: taking without giving back is selfish, importance of contribution and sharing, gratitude and responsibility in life, balance between receiving and giving, selfish behavior consequences, giving back to society

sanskrit: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषै । भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥
transliteration: yajña-śiṣṭāśinaḥ santo mucyante sarva-kilbiṣaiḥ bhuñjate te tv aghaṁ pāpā ye pacanty ātma-kāraṇāt

meanings.english: Those who eat food offered in sacrifice are freed from all sins, but those who cook only for themselves consume sin.
meanings.hindi: जो लोग यज्ञ में अर्पित भोजन को ग्रहण करते हैं, वे सभी पापों से मुक्त हो जाते हैं। लेकिन जो केवल अपने लिए ही पकाते हैं, वे पाप का भोग करते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jo log sharing aur gratitude ke saath khate hain wo free ho jate hain, lekin jo sirf apne liye hi jeete hain wo negativity create karte hain.
meanings.marathi: जे लोक यज्ञातून उरलेले अन्न ग्रहण करतात ते पापांपासून मुक्त होतात, पण जे फक्त स्वतःसाठीच अन्न बनवतात ते पाप भोगतात.

core_meaning: Living with gratitude and sharing purifies, while selfish living leads to negativity and inner burden
use_cases: importance of gratitude in daily life
learning to live with purpose and contribution
embedding_text: gratitude and sharing in life, selfless living vs selfish behavior, importance of contribution and purity, feeling empty despite material success, giving and receiving balance, living with purpose and gratitude

sanskrit: अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः । यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥
transliteration: annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ yajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ

meanings.english: All beings depend on food, food is produced by rain, rain is produced by sacrifice (selfless action), and sacrifice arises from action.
meanings.hindi: सभी प्राणी अन्न पर निर्भर हैं, अन्न वर्षा से उत्पन्न होता है, वर्षा यज्ञ (निःस्वार्थ कर्म) से होती है, और यज्ञ कर्म से उत्पन्न होता है।
meanings.hinglish: Sab kuch ek cycle me connected hai — food, rain, action aur selfless work. Sab ek dusre par depend karte hain.
meanings.marathi: सर्व जीव अन्नावर अवलंबून आहेत, अन्न पावसातून येते, पाऊस यज्ञातून येतो, आणि यज्ञ कर्मातून उत्पन्न होतो.

core_meaning: Life operates in an interconnected cycle where selfless actions sustain the entire system
use_cases: understanding how everything in life is connected
realizing impact of actions on environment and society
learning responsibility towards nature and society
thinking about long-term consequences of actions
embedding_text: everything in life is interconnected cycle, actions impact environment and society, importance of balance and ecosystem, responsibility towards nature, cause and effect in life, how actions sustain the world

sanskrit: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् । तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
transliteration: karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣara-samudbhavam tasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣṭhitam

meanings.english: Know that action arises from the Vedas (divine knowledge), and the Vedas originate from the eternal Supreme. Therefore, the all-pervading Divine is always present in acts of sacrifice (selfless action).
meanings.hindi: जानो कि कर्म वेदों से उत्पन्न होता है, और वेद उस परम अक्षर ब्रह्म से उत्पन्न होते हैं। इसलिए सर्वव्यापी ब्रह्म सदैव यज्ञ (निःस्वार्थ कर्म) में स्थित रहता है।
meanings.hinglish: Samjho ki action divine knowledge se aata hai, aur wo ultimate truth se originate hota hai. Isliye selfless action me hi divine presence hoti hai.
meanings.marathi: कर्म वेदांपासून उत्पन्न होते आणि वेद परम अक्षर ब्रह्मापासून येतात. त्यामुळे सर्वव्यापी ब्रह्म नेहमी यज्ञामध्ये (निःस्वार्थ कर्मात) स्थित असतो.

core_meaning: Selfless action is not just practical—it is deeply connected to divine truth and universal order
use_cases: wanting spiritual connection in daily life
exploring connection between work and spirituality
embedding_text: connection between work and spirituality, selfless action as divine path, finding purpose in daily work, spiritual meaning of actions, higher truth behind actions, connecting life with universal principles

sanskrit: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः । अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥
transliteration: evaṁ pravartitaṁ cakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ aghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati

meanings.english: One who does not follow this cycle of duty and selfless action lives in vain, indulging only in sensory pleasures and leading a sinful life.
meanings.hindi: जो व्यक्ति इस कर्मचक्र का पालन नहीं करता और केवल इन्द्रियों के सुख में लिप्त रहता है, वह व्यर्थ जीवन जीता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan life ke is natural cycle ko follow nahi karta aur sirf pleasures ke peeche bhaagta hai, uski life meaningless ho jaati hai.
meanings.marathi: जो मनुष्य या चक्राचे पालन करत नाही आणि फक्त इंद्रिय सुखांमध्ये रमतो, तो व्यर्थ जीवन जगतो.

core_meaning: Ignoring one's duties and living only for pleasure leads to a meaningless and unfulfilled life
embedding_text: living without purpose leads to emptiness, chasing pleasure without meaning, lack of direction in life, meaningless lifestyle realization, need for purpose and responsibility, avoiding duty leads to dissatisfaction

sanskrit: यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः । आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥
transliteration: yas tv ātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛptaś ca mānavaḥ ātmany eva ca santuṣṭas tasya kāryaṁ na vidyate

meanings.english: But one who is satisfied in the self, content within, and fulfilled by the self alone has no duties to perform.
meanings.hindi: जो व्यक्ति आत्मा में ही आनंदित, संतुष्ट और तृप्त है, उसके लिए कोई कर्तव्य शेष नहीं रहता।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan andar se hi khush aur satisfied hai, usko bahar kuch prove karne ki zarurat nahi hoti.
meanings.marathi: जो मनुष्य आत्म्यातच आनंदी आणि संतुष्ट असतो, त्याच्यासाठी कोणतेही कर्तव्य उरत नाही.

core_meaning: True fulfillment comes from inner contentment, not external achievements or actions
use_cases: wanting to be content without external validation
searching for true satisfaction in life
embedding_text: inner peace and self satisfaction, finding happiness within, not dependent on external success, contentment and fulfillment in life, emotional independence, self realization and inner joy

sanskrit: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन । न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥
transliteration: naiva tasya kṛtenārtho nākṛteneha kaścana na cāsya sarva-bhūteṣu kaścid artha-vyapāśrayaḥ

meanings.english: For one who is self-realized, there is no purpose in performing or avoiding actions, and such a person is not dependent on anyone or anything.
meanings.hindi: जो आत्मज्ञानी है, उसके लिए न तो कर्म करने से कोई लाभ है और न ही कर्म न करने से। वह किसी पर निर्भर नहीं रहता।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan truly self-realized hai, uske liye na kaam karna matter karta hai na na karna, aur wo kisi par dependent nahi hota.
meanings.marathi: जो आत्मज्ञानी आहे, त्याला कर्म केल्याने किंवा न केल्याने काही फरक पडत नाही आणि तो कोणावरही अवलंबून राहत नाही.

core_meaning: A self-realized person becomes independent of external validation, actions, and dependencies
use_cases: not relying on others for happiness
embedding_text: emotional independence and self reliance, freedom from validation and expectations, not depending on others for happiness, inner strength and self realization, detachment from external outcomes

sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर । असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरूषः ॥
transliteration: tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samācara asakto hy ācaran karma param āpnoti pūruṣaḥ

meanings.english: Therefore, always perform your duties without attachment, for by doing work without attachment, a person attains the highest goal.
meanings.hindi: इसलिए बिना आसक्ति के निरंतर अपने कर्तव्य का पालन करो, क्योंकि ऐसा करने से मनुष्य परम लक्ष्य को प्राप्त करता है।
meanings.hinglish: Isliye bina attachment ke apna kaam karte raho, kyunki aise kaam karne se hi life ka highest goal achieve hota hai.
meanings.marathi: म्हणून आसक्तीशिवाय सतत आपले कर्तव्य करा, कारण अशा प्रकारे कर्म केल्याने मनुष्य सर्वोच्च ध्येय प्राप्त करतो.

core_meaning: Consistent action without attachment leads to ultimate success and fulfillment
use_cases: learning to focus on effort rather than results
embedding_text: consistent action without attachment to results, discipline and focus in work, reducing stress about outcomes, long term success habits, working without expectation, purpose driven action

sanskrit: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः । लोकसङ्ग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
transliteration: karmaṇaiva hi saṁsiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśyan kartum arhasi

meanings.english: Even kings like Janaka attained perfection through action. Therefore, you should perform your duties for the welfare and guidance of the world.
meanings.hindi: राजा जनक जैसे महान व्यक्तियों ने भी कर्म के द्वारा ही सिद्धि प्राप्त की। इसलिए तुम्हें लोकसंग्रह (समाज के कल्याण) के लिए कर्म करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Janak jaise logon ne bhi action se success paayi thi. Isliye tumhe bhi sirf apne liye nahi, balki dusron ke benefit ke liye kaam karna chahiye.
meanings.marathi: जनकांसारख्या महान लोकांनी कर्मानेच सिद्धी प्राप्त केली. त्यामुळे तुम्हीही समाजाच्या कल्याणासाठी कर्म करायला हवे.

core_meaning: True success comes from action, and great individuals act not just for themselves but for the welfare of others
use_cases: wanting to make impact in society
finding purpose in contributing to others
embedding_text: leadership through action and responsibility, working for society and others, purpose beyond self benefit, inspiring others through actions, contribution and service mindset, meaningful success through impact

sanskrit: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः । स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥
transliteration: yad yad ācarati śreṣṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate

meanings.english: Whatever a great person does, others follow. Whatever standards they set, the world pursues.
meanings.hindi: श्रेष्ठ व्यक्ति जो भी आचरण करता है, सामान्य लोग उसका अनुसरण करते हैं। वह जो भी मानक स्थापित करता है, लोग उसी का पालन करते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jo bhi ek great person karta hai, log usko follow karte hain. Jo standards wo set karta hai, wahi duniya follow karti hai.
meanings.marathi: श्रेष्ठ व्यक्ती जे काही करतात, तेच इतर लोक अनुसरतात. त्यांनी जे मानक ठरवले, तेच लोक पाळतात.

core_meaning: Leaders influence others through their actions; people follow behavior, not just words
use_cases: being a role model for others
leadership responsibility in workplace or family
embedding_text: leadership through actions and role modeling, influence of behavior on others, people follow actions not words, setting standards and examples, responsibility of leaders and influencers, impact of personal behavior

sanskrit: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन । नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
transliteration: na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣu lokeṣu kiñcana nānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva ca karmaṇi

meanings.english: O Arjuna, there is nothing in all the three worlds that I must do, nor is there anything unattained that I need to attain—yet I continue to engage in action.
meanings.hindi: हे पार्थ, तीनों लोकों में मेरे लिए कोई कर्तव्य नहीं है और न ही ऐसा कुछ है जिसे मुझे प्राप्त करना है, फिर भी मैं कर्म करता रहता हूँ।
meanings.hinglish: Krishna keh rahe hain ki unhe kuch bhi paana ya karna zaroori nahi hai, phir bhi wo action lete rehte hain.
meanings.marathi: हे पार्थ, तीनही लोकांमध्ये माझ्यासाठी काहीही कर्तव्य नाही, आणि मला काहीही मिळवायचे उरलेले नाही, तरीही मी कर्म करत राहतो.

core_meaning: Even when nothing is required, the wise continue to act out of responsibility and purpose, not necessity
use_cases: lack of motivation after reaching goals
acting out of responsibility, not need
embedding_text: working even after success, purpose beyond achievement, continuing action without personal need, discipline and responsibility in life, why keep working after achieving goals, selfless action mindset

sanskrit: यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
transliteration: yadi hy ahaṁ na varteyaṁ jātu karmaṇy atandritaḥ mama vartmānuvartante manuṣyāḥ pārtha sarvaśaḥ

meanings.english: If I were ever to stop performing actions diligently, O Arjuna, people would follow my path in all respects.
meanings.hindi: हे पार्थ, यदि मैं कभी कर्म करने में आलस्य करूँ या रुक जाऊँ, तो सभी लोग मेरे मार्ग का अनुसरण करेंगे।
meanings.hinglish: Agar main action lena band kar doon ya lazy ho jaun, toh log bhi wahi follow karenge.
meanings.marathi: हे पार्थ, जर मी कर्म करणे थांबवले किंवा आळशी झालो, तर लोकही माझ्या मार्गाचे अनुसरण करतील.

core_meaning: Leaders must act consistently because others imitate their behavior
use_cases: impact of personal discipline on others
embedding_text: leaders must act consistently because people follow them, responsibility of role models, importance of discipline and consistency, influence of actions on others, accountability in leadership behavior

sanskrit: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् । सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥
transliteration: utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṁ karma ced aham saṅkarasya ca kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ

meanings.english: If I did not perform my duties, all these worlds would fall into ruin. I would be the cause of chaos and the destruction of society.
meanings.hindi: यदि मैं कर्म न करूँ, तो ये सभी लोक नष्ट हो जाएँगे। मैं अव्यवस्था और समाज के पतन का कारण बन जाऊँगा।
meanings.hinglish: Agar main apna kaam na karun, toh pura system collapse ho jayega aur chaos create ho jayega.
meanings.marathi: जर मी कर्म केले नाही, तर हे सर्व लोक नष्ट होतील आणि समाजात गोंधळ निर्माण होईल.

core_meaning: When responsible individuals stop acting, it leads to chaos and breakdown of the system
use_cases: system collapse due to lack of action
embedding_text: importance of responsibility in leadership, consequences of not taking action, system collapse due to negligence, chaos from lack of discipline, accountability and duty in society, impact of inaction

sanskrit: सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत । कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसङ्ग्रहम् ॥
transliteration: saktāḥ karmaṇy avidvāṁso yathā kurvanti bhārata kuryād vidvāṁs tathāsaktaś cikīrṣur loka-saṅgraham

meanings.english: Just as the ignorant work with attachment, the wise should also act, but without attachment, for the welfare of society.
meanings.hindi: हे भारत, जैसे अज्ञानी लोग आसक्ति के साथ कर्म करते हैं, वैसे ही ज्ञानी भी कर्म करें, लेकिन बिना आसक्ति के, समाज के कल्याण के लिए।
meanings.hinglish: Jaise normal log attachment ke saath kaam karte hain, waise hi wise log bhi kaam karein, par bina attachment ke aur dusron ke benefit ke liye.
meanings.marathi: अज्ञानी लोक आसक्तीने कर्म करतात, तसेच ज्ञानी लोकांनीही कर्म करावे, पण आसक्तीशिवाय आणि समाजाच्या हितासाठी.

core_meaning: The wise should act like others but with awareness and without attachment, guiding society through their actions
use_cases: working in society while staying detached
balancing personal growth and social responsibility
being part of system but not attached to it
embedding_text: acting in society without attachment, leadership through example, working for others benefit without ego, balancing awareness and action, guiding others through behavior, detached involvement in work

sanskrit: न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् । जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्तः समाचरन् ॥
transliteration: na buddhi-bhedaṁ janayed ajñānāṁ karma-saṅginām joṣayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācaran

meanings.english: The wise should not disturb the understanding of those attached to action. Instead, they should encourage them to perform their duties properly by setting an example.
meanings.hindi: ज्ञानी व्यक्ति को कर्म में लगे अज्ञानियों की बुद्धि को विचलित नहीं करना चाहिए, बल्कि स्वयं उदाहरण बनकर उन्हें सही ढंग से कर्म करने के लिए प्रेरित करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Wise logon ko dusron ko confuse nahi karna chahiye, balki khud example set karke unhe sahi tarike se kaam karne ke liye inspire karna chahiye.
meanings.marathi: ज्ञानी व्यक्तीने अज्ञान लोकांची बुद्धी विचलित करू नये, तर स्वतः उदाहरण देऊन त्यांना योग्य प्रकारे कर्म करण्यास प्रेरित करावे.
