meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan devotion ke saath kaam karta hai, jiska mann saaf hai aur jisne apni indriyaan (senses) jeet li hain, wo sabka pyaara ban jata hai. Aisa insaan kaam karte hue bhi uske chakkar me nahi fasta.
meanings.marathi: जो ईश्वरी भक्तीने कर्म करतो, ज्याचे अंतःकरण शुद्ध आहे आणि ज्याने आपले मन व इंद्रिये जिंकली आहेत, तो सर्वांना प्रिय होतो आणि सर्व त्याला प्रिय होतात. असा मनुष्य कर्मे करूनही त्यात लिप्त होत नाही.

core_meaning: The Social and Spiritual Impact of Self-Control: A disciplined and pure-hearted person radiates peace and remains free from the 'sticky' reactions of daily work.
use_cases: improving professional relationships through a calm demeanor
working in high-stress environments without burning out
becoming a person of integrity whom everyone trusts
avoiding the guilt or stress associated with results
embedding_text: work in devotion, pure soul, controls senses, dear to everyone, never entangled in work, yoga-yukto

sanskrit:
नैव किञ्चित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् |
पश्यञ्श‍ृण्वन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्न‍न्गच्छन्स्वपन्श्वसन ॥ ८ ॥
प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि |
इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन् ॥ ९ ॥
transliteration:
naiva kiñcit karomīti yukto manyeta tattva-vit
paśyañ śṛṇvan spṛśañ jighrann aśnan gacchan svapañ śvasan
pralapan visṛjan gṛhṇann unmiṣan nimiṣann api
indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu vartanta iti dhārayan

meanings.english: A person in divine consciousness, though engaged in seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, moving, sleeping, and breathing, knows that they actually do nothing at all. Even while speaking, giving, receiving, or blinking, they understand that only the senses are occupied with their objects and that the true Self remains aloof.
meanings.hindi: तत्त्व को जानने वाला सांख्ययोगी देखते, सुनते, स्पर्श करते, सूँघते, खाते, चलते, सोते और श्वास लेते हुए भी यही माने कि 'मैं कुछ भी नहीं करता हूँ'। बोलते, त्यागते, ग्रहण करते तथा आँखों को खोलते-मूंदते हुए भी वह यही समझे कि इन्द्रियाँ ही अपने अर्थों (विषयों) में बरत रही हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan sachai ko jaanta hai, wo dekhte, sunte, chalte ya saans lete waqt bhi ye jaanta hai ki wo khud kuch nahi kar raha. Wo samajhta hai ki ye sab sirf senses (indriya) apna kaam kar rahi hain aur wo inse alag hai.
meanings.marathi: तत्त्ववेत्ता पुरुष पाहणे, ऐकणे, स्पर्श करणे, वास घेणे, जेवणे, चालणे, झोपणे आणि श्वास घेणे या क्रिया करत असतानाही 'मी काहीच करत नाही' असे मानतो. बोलणे, त्यागणे, ग्रहण करणे किंवा डोळे उघडझाप करणे या क्रियांवेळीही केवळ इंद्रियेच आपापल्या विषयात वावरत आहेत, अशी त्याची धारणा असते.

core_meaning: The Observer Mindset: Real liberation comes from realizing that physical and biological functions are mechanical processes of the body/senses, while the conscious 'I' remains a detached observer (Sakshi).
use_cases: reducing performance anxiety by viewing actions as natural functions of skill and body
managing sensory overload by reminding yourself 'I am not these senses'
breaking the 'doer' complex (ego) that leads to stress and burnout
practicing mindfulness during mundane tasks like eating or walking
staying calm in high-pressure environments by staying 'aloof' internally
embedding_text: I do nothing at all, senses engaged with objects, self is aloof, tattva-vit, divine consciousness

sanskrit: ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति य: । लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥ १० ॥
transliteration: brahmaṇy ādhāya karmāṇi saṅgaṁ tyaktvā karoti yaḥ lipyate na sa pāpena padma-patram ivāmbhasā

meanings.english: One who performs his duty without attachment, surrendering the results unto the Supreme Lord, is unaffected by sinful action, as the lotus leaf is untouched by water.
meanings.hindi: जो व्यक्ति कर्मफल को परमेश्वर को समर्पित करके आसक्ति रहित होकर अपना कर्तव्य निभाता है, वह पाप से उसी प्रकार अप्रभावित रहता है जैसे कमल का पत्ता जल से नहीं भीगता।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan apna farz bina kisi lalach ke nibhaata hai aur sab kuch God ko dedicate kar deta hai, use koi bhi burai chhu nahi paati—theek waise hi jaise kamal (lotus) ka patta pani me rehkar bhi geela nahi hota.
meanings.marathi: जो मनुष्य सर्व कर्मे परमेश्वराला अर्पण करून आणि आसक्ती सोडून कर्तव्य करतो, तो पापाने लिप्त होत नाही; ज्याप्रमाणे कमळाचे पान पाण्यात असूनही त्याला पाणी लागत नाही.

core_meaning: The Lotus Strategy: Living in the world but remaining untouched by its negativity through the power of detachment and surrender.
use_cases: staying positive in a toxic workplace
dealing with failure or criticism without losing self-worth
performing duties without becoming 'obsessed' with the outcome
navigating complex social situations while maintaining values
visualizing a state of emotional immunity
embedding_text: lotus leaf untouched by water, perform duty without attachment, surrender results to Supreme, unaffected by sin

sanskrit: कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि । योगिन: कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वात्म‍श‍ुद्धये ॥ ११ ॥
transliteration: kāyena manasā buddhyā kevalair indriyair api yoginaḥ karma kurvanti saṅgaṁ tyaktvātma-śuddhaye

meanings.english: The yogīs, abandoning attachment, act with body, mind, intelligence and even with the senses, only for the purpose of purification.
meanings.hindi: योगी जन आसक्ति का त्याग करके केवल शुद्धि के लिए शरीर, मन, बुद्धि तथा इन्द्रियों के द्वारा कर्म करते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Yogis apne dimaag, body aur akal se kaam toh karte hain, par unka motive sirf khud ko andar se saaf (purify) karna hota hai, na ki results ka maza lena.
meanings.marathi: योगी पुरुष आसक्तीचा त्याग करून केवळ अंतःकरण शुद्धीसाठी शरीर, मन, बुद्धी आणि इंद्रियांच्या द्वारे कर्मे करत असतात.

core_meaning: Work as Therapy: Actions performed without greed act as a 'deep cleanse' for the mind and soul, transforming labor into self-growth.
use_cases: viewing daily chores as a form of mindfulness practice
using one's career as a tool for personal development rather than just money
recovering from a period of mental clutter or bad habits
embedding_text: act with body mind intelligence for purification, abandoning attachment, atma-shuddhaye, work for self-cleansing

sanskrit: युक्त: कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्‍नोति नैष्ठिकीम् । अयुक्त: कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥ १२ ॥
transliteration: yuktaḥ karma-phalaṁ tyaktvā śāntim āpnoti naiṣṭhikīm ayuktaḥ kāma-kāreṇa phale sakto nibadhyate

meanings.english: The steadily devoted soul attains unadulterated peace because he offers the result of all activities to Me; whereas a person who is not in union with the Divine, who is greedy for the fruits of his labor, becomes entangled.
meanings.hindi: भक्ति में लगा हुआ व्यक्ति कर्मफल का त्याग करके निश्चल शांति प्राप्त करता है, किन्तु जो व्यक्ति भगवान से नहीं जुड़ा है और फलों का लालची है, वह अपने ही कर्मों में फँस जाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan God se juda hai, wo result ki chinta chhod kar gehri shanti paata hai. Par jo laalchi hai aur results me fasa rehta hai, wo hamesha pareshaan rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: युक्त (योगी) कर्माच्या फळाचा त्याग करून शाश्वत शांती प्राप्त करतो; परंतु अयुक्त (जो परमेश्वराशी जोडला गेलेला नाही) फळाच्या ठिकाणी आसक्त असल्यामुळे वासनेने बद्ध होतो.

core_meaning: The Price of Greed vs. The Reward of Devotion: Attachment to results causes anxiety and bondage, while offering results brings deep, lasting peace.
use_cases: curing the 'what if' anxiety regarding exams or projects
breaking free from the cycle of comparison and envy
experiencing 'unadulterated peace' in the middle of a busy schedule
understanding why some successful people are still deeply unhappy
embedding_text: devoted soul attains peace by giving up fruits, greedy person gets entangled, anxiety vs peace, attached to results

sanskrit:
सर्वकर्माणि मनसा सन्न्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी |
नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥ १३ ॥
transliteration:
sarva-karmāṇi manasā sannyasyāste sukhaṁ vaśī
nava-dvāre pure dehī naiva kurvan na kārayan

meanings.english: When the embodied living being controls his nature and mentally renounces all actions, he resides happily in the city of nine gates [the body], neither working nor causing work to be done.
meanings.hindi: जब वश में किया हुआ जीवात्मा मन से सब कर्मों का त्याग कर देता है, तब वह नौ द्वारों वाले इस शरीर रूपी नगर में न कुछ करता हुआ और न कुछ करवाता हुआ आनंदपूर्वक रहता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jab insaan apne dimaag se sab karma tyag deta hai, wo is 9 darwazo wale sheher (body) me bade araam se rehta hai. Wo na kuch karta hai na karwata hai, bas shanti se rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्या संयमी पुरुषाने सर्व कर्मे मनाने त्यागली आहेत, तो नऊ दारे असलेल्या या देहरूपी नगरात काहीही न करता आणि न करविता आनंदाने राहतो.

core_meaning: The City of Nine Gates: The body is viewed as a physical dwelling with nine openings (eyes, ears, nostrils, mouth, etc.). The soul is merely the tenant, staying happily detached while the biological city runs its own course.
use_cases: visualizing yourself as a calm passenger in the vehicle of the body
reducing the stress of physical ailments by distancing the 'self' from the 'body'
practicing mental renunciation even while physically busy with chores
understanding that biological processes (breathing, digestion) are automatic and don't require ego
finding inner quietness amidst the 'noise' of physical senses
embedding_text: city of nine gates, nava-dvare pure, mental renunciation, resides happily

sanskrit:
न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभु: |
न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥ १४ ॥
transliteration:
na kartṛtvaṁ na karmāṇi lokasya sṛjati prabhuḥ
na karma-phala-saṁyogam svabhāvas tu pravartate

meanings.english: The embodied spirit, master of the body, does not create activities, nor proprietorship, nor the fruits of action. All this is enacted by the modes of material nature (svabhāva).
meanings.hindi: शरीर का स्वामी जीवात्मा न तो कर्मों को उत्पन्न करता है, न लोगों को कर्म करने की प्रेरणा देता है और न ही कर्मों के फल का संयोग करता है; यह सब तो प्रकृति के गुणों (स्वभाव) द्वारा ही किया जाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Soul na toh kaam create karti hai, na hi result se uska koi lena dena hai. Ye sab toh 'Nature' (Svabhava) ka khel hai jo apne aap chalta rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: देहाचा स्वामी आत्मा ना कर्मे निर्माण करतो, ना लोकांना कर्माला प्रवृत्त करतो, ना कर्मफळाचा संयोग घडवून आणतो. हे सर्व निसर्गाच्या गुणांद्वारे (स्वभाव) घडत असते.

core_meaning: The Non-Doer: The soul isn't the manager or the creator of outcomes. Everything—from your heartbeat to your career milestones—is a result of the interaction between material nature's laws (Gunas).
use_cases: letting go of the 'God complex' where you feel responsible for everything in your life
understanding that 'nature' handles the results, so you can focus purely on effort
analyzing your 'svabhava' (nature) to understand why you behave in certain ways
reducing guilt over things that were governed by external circumstances
observing the laws of physics and biology as the real 'workers' in the body
embedding_text: no proprietorship of action, svabhava acts, master of the city does not create fruits

sanskrit:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभु: |
अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तव: ॥ १५ ॥
transliteration:
nādatte kasyacit pāpaṁ na caiva sukṛtaṁ vibhuḥ
ajñānenāvṛtaṁ jñānaṁ tena muhyanti jantavaḥ

meanings.english: The Supreme Lord does not assume anyone’s sinful or pious activities. Living beings are bewildered because their real knowledge is covered by ignorance.
meanings.hindi: परमेश्वर न किसी के पाप कर्म को और न किसी के पुण्य कर्म को ग्रहण करता है। अज्ञान द्वारा ज्ञान ढका हुआ है, इसी से सब जीव मोहित हो रहे हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Bhagwan na toh tumhare paap lete hain na punya. Sab kuch dimaag me chapi 'Ignorance' (Agyan) ki wajah se hai jo hamare asli gyan ko dhak deti hai.
meanings.marathi: परमेश्वर कोणाचेही पाप किंवा पुण्य स्वीकारत नाही. अज्ञानाने ज्ञान झाकलेले असल्यामुळे सर्व प्राणी मोहग्रस्त होतात.

core_meaning: The Neutrality of the Divine: God doesn't micromanage your 'moral bank account' or take responsibility for your choices. Suffering exists only because ignorance (Maya) clouds our inherent wisdom.
use_cases: taking 100% personal responsibility for your life's direction
recognizing that 'blaming God' for misfortune is a sign of ignorance
removing the 'veils' of bias and ignorance through constant learning
understanding the concept of free will within a neutral universe
striving to uncover the inner wisdom that is currently hidden by temporary confusion
embedding_text: Lord assumes no sin or piety, knowledge covered by ignorance, vibhu is neutral

sanskrit: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मन: । तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥ १६ ॥
transliteration: jñānena tu tad ajñānaṁ yeṣāṁ nāśitam ātmanaḥ teṣām āditya-vaj jñānaṁ prakāśayati tat param

meanings.english: When, however, one is enlightened with the knowledge by which nescience is destroyed, then his knowledge reveals everything, as the sun lights up everything in the daytime.
meanings.hindi: किन्तु जब कोई उस ज्ञान से प्रबुद्ध होता है जिससे अविद्या का विनाश होता है, तो उसका वह ज्ञान सब कुछ उसी प्रकार प्रकट कर देता है जैसे दिन में सूर्य सारी वस्तुओं को प्रकाशित कर देता है।
meanings.hinglish: Lekin jab insaan ko wo saccha gyaan mil jata hai jisse andhera (ignorance) khatam ho jaye, toh sab kuch ekdum clear ho jata hai—waise hi jaise suraj nikalne par sab dikhne lagta hai.
meanings.marathi: परंतु ज्यांचे ते अज्ञान आत्मज्ञानाने नष्ट झाले आहे, त्यांचे ते ज्ञान सूर्याप्रमाणे त्या परमात्म्याला प्रकाशित करते.

core_meaning: The Sunrise of Knowledge: True spiritual wisdom doesn't just add information; it removes the 'darkness' of confusion, making the truth self-evident.
use_cases: overcoming a long-standing state of confusion or 'feeling lost'
gaining the clarity needed to make a life-changing decision
seeing through the illusions of temporary material happiness
realizing one's true identity beyond the physical body
finding hope after a period of mental darkness
embedding_text: knowledge destroys ignorance like the sun, enlightenment reveals everything, aditya-vat jñānam, spiritual sun

sanskrit: तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणा: । गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषा: ॥ १७ ॥
transliteration: tad-buddhayas tad-ātmānas tan-niṣṭhās tat-parāyaṇāḥ gacchanty apunar-āvṛttiṁ jñāna-nirdhūta-kalmaṣāḥ

meanings.english: When one’s intelligence, mind, faith and refuge are all fixed in the Supreme, then one becomes fully cleansed of misgivings through complete knowledge and thus proceeds straight on the path of liberation.
meanings.hindi: जब मनुष्य की बुद्धि, मन, श्रद्धा तथा शरण सब कुछ भगवान में स्थिर हो जाते हैं, तब वह पूर्ण ज्ञान के द्वारा समस्त संशयों से शुद्ध होकर मुक्ति के मार्ग पर आगे बढ़ता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jab tumhari buddhi, mann, bharosa aur sahara sab kuch us Param Pita (God) me lag jata hai, tab tumhare saare doubts khatam ho jate hain aur tum freedom (liberation) ki taraf nikal padte ho.
meanings.marathi: ज्याची बुद्धी, मन, निष्ठा आणि आश्रय हे सर्व त्या परमात्म्यामध्येच एकरूप झाले आहेत, त्याची सर्व पापे ज्ञानाने धुवून निघतात आणि तो पुनरावृत्तीरहित अशा मोक्षाला प्राप्त होतो.

core_meaning: Total Alignment: When every faculty of the human psyche (intellect, emotion, and faith) is synchronized with the Divine, the 'mental fog' of doubt vanishes.
use_cases: finding a singular, unwavering life purpose
cleansing the mind of negative past impressions
achieving a state of total mental integration
moving from confusion to absolute conviction
embedding_text: intelligence and mind fixed on Supreme, cleansed of misgivings, path of liberation, total alignment with God

sanskrit: विद्याविनयसम्पन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि । श‍ुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिता: समदर्शिन: ॥ १८ ॥
transliteration: vidyā-vinaya-sampanne brāhmaṇe gavi hastini śuni caiva śva-pāke ca paṇḍitāḥ sama-darśinaḥ

meanings.english: The humble sages, by virtue of true knowledge, see with equal vision a learned and gentle brāhmaṇa, a cow, an elephant, a dog and a dog-eater [outcaste].
meanings.hindi: विनम्र साधु पुरुष अपने वास्तविक ज्ञान के कारण एक विद्वान ब्राह्मण, गाय, हाथी, कुत्ता तथा चण्डाल को समान दृष्टि से देखते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jo log sach me samajhdaar hote hain, wo sabko ek barabar dekhte hain—chahe wo koi bada scholar ho, koi janwar ho, ya koi aisa insaan jise samaj ne thukra diya ho. Wo sabke andar ki soul ko pehchante hain.
meanings.marathi: खरे ज्ञानी लोक विद्या आणि विनय यांनी युक्त असलेला ब्राह्मण, गाय, हत्ती, कुत्रा आणि चांडाळ या सर्वांच्या ठिकाणी समदृष्टी ठेवतात.

core_meaning: Spiritual Equality: True wisdom is the ability to see the same divine spark in all living beings, regardless of their external 'packaging' (species, status, or education).
use_cases: eliminating prejudices and biases based on race, class, or religion
developing deep empathy for animals and nature
cultivating a non-judgmental attitude toward others
embedding_text: equal vision, seeing soul in all beings, no distinction between castes or species, sama-darshinah

sanskrit:
इहैव तैर्जित: सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मन: |
निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्‍ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिता: ॥ १९ ॥
transliteration:
ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣāṁ sāmye sthitaṁ manaḥ
nirdoṣaṁ hi samaṁ brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ

meanings.english: Those whose minds are established in equanimity have already conquered the cycle of birth and death in this very life. Since Brahman is flawless and equal to all, they are already situated in Brahman.
meanings.hindi: जिनका मन समभाव (equanimity) में स्थित है, उन्होंने इसी जीवन में जन्म-मृत्यु के चक्र को जीत लिया है। चूँकि ब्रह्म निर्दोष और सबके लिए समान है, इसलिए वे ब्रह्म में ही स्थित हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jinka mann hamesha shant aur barabar (balanced) rehta hai, unhone jeete ji hi sansar ko jeet liya hai. Wo log bilkul flawless hote hain aur bhagwan me hi rehte hain.
meanings.marathi: ज्यांचे मन समत्वामध्ये (समानतेमध्ये) स्थिर झाले आहे, त्यांनी या लोकीच जन्म-मृत्यूवर विजय मिळवला आहे. ब्रह्म हे निर्दोष आणि सम आहे, म्हणून असे पुरुष ब्रह्माच्या ठिकाणीच स्थित असतात.

core_meaning: Liberation While Living: Spiritual victory isn't something that happens after death; it happens the moment you achieve 'Samya' (mental equilibrium). To be unbiased and equal to all is to be divine.
use_cases: achieving a state where praise and criticism no longer shake your confidence
realizing that internal balance is the ultimate form of success
treating friends and rivals with the same professional integrity
conquering the fear of the future by staying grounded in present mental peace
overcoming the 'rollercoaster' of emotions caused by external wins and losses
embedding_text: conquered birth and death in this life, mind in equanimity, flawless like Brahman

sanskrit: न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् । स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद्‍ब्रह्मणि स्थित: ॥ २० ॥
transliteration: na prahṛṣyet priyaṁ prāpya nodvijet prāpya cāpriyam sthira-buddhir asammūḍho brahma-vid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ

meanings.english: A person who neither rejoices upon achieving something pleasant nor laments upon obtaining something unpleasant, who is self-intelligent, who is unbewildered, and who knows the science of God is already situated in transcendence.
meanings.hindi: जो व्यक्ति न तो कुछ प्रिय पाने पर हर्षित होता है और न ही अप्रिय पाने पर विचलित होता है, जो स्थिरबुद्धि, मोह-रहित और भगवद्-भक्त है, वह पहले से ही दिव्य स्थिति में स्थित है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo achha hone par bahut zyada khush nahi hota aur bura hone par dukhi hokar toot nahi jata, wahi asli samajhdaar hai. Aisa insaan hamesha balance me rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याला प्रिय गोष्ट मिळाल्यावर आनंद होत नाही आणि अप्रिय गोष्ट मिळाल्यावर दुःख होत नाही, असा स्थिर बुद्धीचा आणि मोहविरहित पुरुष ब्रह्मस्थितीमध्ये स्थिर आहे असे समजावे.

core_meaning: Equanimity (Sthira-buddhi): Being unshakeable. Not letting the 'highs' of success or 'lows' of failure dictate your internal state.
use_cases: managing emotional volatility and mood swings
handling criticism and praise with the same grace
staying calm during market fluctuations or professional uncertainty
maintaining focus regardless of external circumstances
embedding_text: neither rejoices nor laments, steady intelligence, sthira-buddhi, unbewildered in pleasant and unpleasant

sanskrit:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् |
स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्न‍ुते ॥ २१ ॥
transliteration:
bāhya-sparśeṣv asaktātmā vindaty ātmani yat sukham
sa brahma-yoga-yuktātmā sukham akṣayam aśnute

meanings.english: One who is not attached to external sense pleasures finds happiness within the Self. Being united with the Supreme through yoga, such a person enjoys unlimited, inexhaustible happiness.
meanings.hindi: जो बाहरी इन्द्रिय-सुखों में आसक्त नहीं होता, वह अपने आप में (आत्मा में) सुख प्राप्त करता है। ऐसा व्यक्ति ब्रह्म-योग में लीन होकर अक्षय (कभी न खत्म होने वाले) आनंद का अनुभव करता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo bahar ki cheezon aur maze (external pleasures) me nahi fasta, use apne andar hi asli khushi mil jati hai. Bhagwan se jud kar wo aisi khushi paata hai jo kabhi khatam nahi hoti.
meanings.marathi: ज्याचे अंतःकरण बाह्य विषयांच्या सुखात आसक्त नाही, त्याला आत्म्याच्या ठिकाणी जे सुख मिळते, तो ब्रह्मयोगाशी एकरूप झालेला पुरुष अक्षय सुखाचा उपभोग घेतो.

core_meaning: The Internal Upgrade: True happiness isn't found by upgrading your external lifestyle, but by accessing the 'Atma-sukham' (joy of the soul). When you find a higher inner taste, the temporary charm of the outside world naturally fades away.
use_cases: overcoming addictive behaviors or cheap dopamine hits (like endless scrolling)
finding mental peace that doesn't depend on your bank balance or social status
realizing that lasting satisfaction comes from 'being' rather than 'consuming'
staying focused on deep, meaningful work instead of surface-level distractions
developing an 'internal compass' for happiness that is immune to external praise or hate
embedding_text: not attached to external sense pleasure, happiness within self, unlimited happiness, brahma-yoga

sanskrit: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दु:खयोनय एव ते । आद्यन्तवन्त: कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुध: ॥ २२ ॥
transliteration: ye hi saṁsparśa-jā bhogā duḥkha-yonaya eva te ādy-antavantaḥ kaunteya na teषु ramate budhaḥ

meanings.english: An intelligent person does not take part in the sources of misery, which are due to contact with the material senses. O son of Kuntī, such pleasures have a beginning and an end, and so the wise man does not delight in them.
meanings.hindi: इन्द्रियों के स्पर्श से उत्पन्न होने वाले भोग दुःख के कारण हैं। हे कुन्तीपुत्र! ऐसे भोगों का आदि और अन्त होता है, अतः बुद्धिमान व्यक्ति उनमें रुचि नहीं लेता।
meanings.hinglish: Indriyon (senses) se milne wale maze asliyat me dukh ki jadd (root) hain. Inka ek start hota hai aur ek end, isliye jo samajhdaar hai wo in temporary cheezon me apna dil nahi lagata.
meanings.marathi: इंद्रिये आणि विषय यांच्या संयोगातून निर्माण होणारे भोग हे दुःखाला कारणीभूत ठरतात. त्यांना सुरुवात आणि अंत असतो, म्हणून शहाणा माणूस त्यात रमत नाही.

core_meaning: The Illusion of Temporary Pleasure: Sensory delights are essentially 'seeds of suffering' because they are temporary and lead to addiction or withdrawal.
use_cases: breaking free from instant gratification and addictions
investing time in long-term satisfaction rather than short-term thrills
embedding_text: sensory pleasures are sources of misery, temporary pleasure has beginning and end, wise man does not delight in senses

sanskrit: शक्न‍ोतीहैव य: सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् । कामक्रोधोद्भ‍वं वेगं स युक्त: स सुखी नर: ॥ २३ ॥
transliteration: śaknotīhaiva yaḥ soḍhuṁ prāk śarīra-vimokṣaṇāt kāma-krodhodbhavaṁ vegaṁ sa yuktaḥ sa sukhī naraḥ

meanings.english: Before giving up this present body, if one is able to tolerate the urges of the material senses and check the force of desire and anger, he is well situated and is happy in this world.
meanings.hindi: यदि इस शरीर को त्यागने से पूर्व कोई मनुष्य इन्द्रियों के वेगों को सहने और इच्छा तथा क्रोध की शक्ति को रोकने में समर्थ हो जाता है, तो वह इस संसार में सुखी है।
meanings.hinglish: Agar tum marne se pehle apni gusse (anger) aur lalach (desire) ki 'force' ko control karna seekh jao, toh tum sach me ek happy insaan ho.
meanings.marathi: देहत्याग करण्यापूर्वी जो मनुष्य काम आणि क्रोध यांच्यापासून उत्पन्न होणारा वेग सहन करण्यास समर्थ होतो, तोच खरा योगी आणि तोच खरा सुखी होय.

core_meaning: Impulse Control: The 'force' of anger and desire is like a physical urge. Happiness is found not in satisfying these urges, but in being strong enough to withstand them.
use_cases: Anger management in personal or professional life
practicing discipline in diet, speech, and habits
embedding_text: tolerate urges of desire and anger, happy in this world, control senses before death, impulse control

sanskrit: योऽन्त:सुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव य: । स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥ २४ ॥
transliteration: yo ’ntaḥ-sukho ’ntar-ārāmas tathāntar-jyotir eva yaḥ sa yogī brahma-nirvāṇaṁ brahma-bhūto ’dhigacchati

meanings.english: One whose happiness is within, who is active and rejoices within, and whose aim is inward is actually the perfect mystic. He is liberated in the Supreme, and ultimately he attains the Supreme.
meanings.hindi: जो अन्तःकरण में ही सुख वाला है, जो अपने भीतर ही रमण करने वाला है और जो भीतर ही ज्ञान रूपी ज्योति वाला है, वह योगी मुक्त है और वह परब्रह्म को प्राप्त होता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan apne andar hi khushi dhoond leta hai, jo apne aap me hi enjoy karta hai aur jiske andar gyaan ki roshni hai, wahi asli yogi hai.
meanings.marathi: जो अंतर्यामीच सुखी आहे, जो आत्म्यामध्येच रममाण होतो आणि ज्याला अंतरात्म्यातच ज्ञानाचा प्रकाश लाभला आहे, तो योगी ब्रह्मरूप होऊन मोक्ष प्राप्त करतो.

core_meaning: Inner Sovereignty: Happiness that doesn't depend on external 'events' or 'people'. The soul becomes its own source of light and joy.
use_cases: finding peace while being alone (solitude vs loneliness)
developing a rich inner life and self-reflection practice
experiencing joy that isn't tied to the material world
embedding_text: happiness within, active and rejoices within, inward aim, brahma-bhuto, inner light

sanskrit: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषय: क्षीणकल्मषा: । छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मान: सर्वभूतहिते रता: ॥ २५ ॥
transliteration: labhante brahma-nirvāṇam ṛṣayaḥ kṣīṇa-kalmaṣāḥ chinna-dvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ

meanings.english: Those who are beyond the dualities that arise from doubts, whose minds are engaged within, who are always busy working for the welfare of all living beings and who are free from all sins achieve liberation in the Supreme.
meanings.hindi: जिनके संशय मिट गए हैं, जिनका मन वश में है और जो समस्त प्राणियों के कल्याण में लगे रहते हैं, वे ऋषि दिव्य पद (मुक्ति) को प्राप्त करते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Wo log jinke doubts khatam ho chuke hain, jo apne mann par control rakhte hain aur jo hamesha doosron ki bhalai ke liye kaam karte hain, unhe hi asli mukti milti hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्यांची पापे नष्ट झाली आहेत, ज्यांचे संशय फिटले आहेत, ज्यांचे मन स्वाधीन आहे आणि जे सर्व प्राण्यांच्या हितामध्ये मग्न आहेत, ते ऋषी मोक्ष प्राप्त करतात.

core_meaning: Altruism as a Spiritual Path: Liberation isn't just for oneself; it is naturally expressed through serving others (Sarva-bhuta-hite-ratah).
use_cases: finding spiritual fulfillment through social work or charity
overcoming selfish tendencies by focusing on global welfare
resolving internal conflicts by helping others
connecting personal growth with planetary healing
embedding_text: working for welfare of all living beings, sarva-bhuta-hite ratah, free from doubts and sins, liberation through service

sanskrit:
कामक्रोधविमुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् |
अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥ २६ ॥
transliteration:
kāma-krodha-vimuktānāṁ yatīnāṁ yata-cetasām
abhito brahma-nirvāṇaṁ vartate viditātmanām

meanings.english: Those who are free from anger and all material desires, who are self-realized, self-disciplined, and constantly striving for perfection, are assured of liberation in the Supreme in the very near future.
meanings.hindi: जो काम और क्रोध से रहित हैं, जिन्होंने अपने मन को जीत लिया है और जो आत्म-साक्षात्कारी हैं, उन प्रयत्नशील योगियों के लिए सब ओर से शांत ब्रह्म-निर्वाण (मुक्ति) ही वर्तमान है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo log gussa (anger) aur lalach (desire) se free hain, jinka apne dimaag par pura control hai, unke liye shanti aur mukti har taraf maujood hai.
meanings.marathi: जे काम आणि क्रोधापासून मुक्त आहेत, ज्यांनी मनावर ताबा मिळवला आहे आणि ज्यांना आत्मज्ञान झाले आहे, अशा प्रयत्नशील पुरुषांना सर्व बाजूंनी परमानंद (ब्रह्मनिर्वाण) प्राप्त झालेलाच असतो.

core_meaning: The Shield of Self-Mastery: Liberation (Brahma-nirvāṇa) is not a distant goal for those who have neutralized the two biggest internal enemies—Desire (Kama) and Anger (Krodha). For a self-governed person, peace exists 'everywhere' and 'right now'.
use_cases: maintaining cool-headedness in a high-stress coding sprint or workplace conflict
recognizing that 'Brahma-nirvāṇa' is the absence of mental misery, not just a physical place
using meditation to 'uproot' deep-seated frustrations before they turn into outbursts
staying focused on a long-term goal without being distracted by immediate cravings
finding a state of 'flow' where external disturbances fail to penetrate your focus
embedding_text: free from anger and desire, self-realized, self-disciplined, brahma-nirvana is everywhere

sanskrit: स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवो: । प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥ २७ ॥ यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायण: । विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो य: सदा मुक्त एव स: ॥ २८ ॥
transliteration: sparśān kṛtvā bahir bāhyāṁś cakṣuś caivāntare bhruvoḥ prāṇāpānau samau kṛtvā nāsābhyantara-cāriṇau yatendriya-mano-buddhir munir mokṣa-parāyaṇaḥ vigatecchā-bhaya-krodho yaḥ sadā mukta eva saḥ

meanings.english: Shutting out all external sense objects, keeping the eyes and vision concentrated between the eyebrows, suspending the breaths within the nostrils, and controlling the mind, senses, and intelligence, the transcendentalist becomes free from desire, fear, and anger. One who is always in this state is certainly liberated.
meanings.hindi: समस्त इन्द्रिय विषयों को बाहर निकालकर, दृष्टि को भृकुटी के मध्य स्थिर करके, नासिका के भीतर प्राण तथा अपान वायु को रोककर, जो अपनी इन्द्रियों, मन तथा बुद्धि को वश में कर लेता है, वह इच्छा, भय तथा क्रोध से रहित होकर सदैव मुक्त रहता है।
meanings.hinglish: Bahari distractions ko rok kar, apni nazar dono eyebrows ke beech tika kar, aur saanson ko balance karke jo apne mann aur dimaag ko kabu kar leta hai, wo darr aur gusse se azad ho jata hai. Aisa insaan hamesha ke liye free (mukta) hai.
meanings.marathi: बाहेरच्या विषयांना बाहेरच ठेवून, दृष्टी भ्रूमध्याच्या ठिकाणी स्थिर करून आणि नासिकापुटातून फिरणारे प्राण व अपान वायू समान करून, ज्याने आपली इंद्रिये, मन व बुद्धी जिंकली आहे, असा मोक्षपरायण मुनी इच्छा, भय व क्रोध यांपासून मुक्त होऊन सदैव मुक्तच असतो.

core_meaning: The Mechanics of Focus: A practical technique for meditation involving sensory withdrawal (Pratyahara) and breath control (Pranayama) to eliminate mental disturbances like fear and anger.
use_cases: practicing deep focus during intense work or study sessions
using breathwork to calm down during an anxiety attack
minimizing environmental distractions to achieve 'flow state'
preparing the mind for deeper spiritual realization
embedding_text: shutting out sense objects, focus between eyebrows, breath control, free from desire fear and anger, liberated state

sanskrit: भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् । सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥ २९ ॥
transliteration: bhoktāraṁ yajña-tapasāṁ sarva-loka-maheśvaram suhṛdaṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ jñātvā māṁ śāntim ṛcchati

meanings.english: A person in full consciousness of Me, knowing Me to be the ultimate beneficiary of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all planets and demigods, and the benefactor and well-wisher of all living entities, attains peace from the pangs of material miseries.
meanings.hindi: मुझे समस्त यज्ञों तथा तपस्याओं का परम भोक्ता, समस्त लोकों तथा देवताओं का परमेश्वर एवं समस्त जीवों का परम हितैषी (मित्र) जानकर, मनुष्य भौतिक कष्टों से शांति प्राप्त करता है।
meanings.hinglish: Ye jaan lo ki Krishna hi saari mehnat aur sacrifices ke asli beneficiary hain, wo pure universe ke maalik hain, aur wo sabse bade well-wisher (dost) hain. Ye samajhte hi insaan ko asli shanti milti hai.
meanings.marathi: मला सर्व यज्ञ आणि तप यांचा उपभोक्ता, सर्व लोकांचा महान ईश्वर आणि सर्व प्राण्यांचा सुहृद (मित्र) असे ओळखून मनुष्य शांती प्राप्त करतो.

core_meaning: The Ultimate Peace Formula: Peace is achieved by acknowledging three truths: God is the ultimate enjoyer, the ultimate proprietor, and the ultimate friend of every soul.
use_cases: finding comfort in the fact that we are never truly alone or friendless
reducing the stress of 'ownership' and the fear of losing things
shifting from a self-centered to a God-centered worldview
ending the struggle for 'control' over life's events
accessing a source of unconditional love and protection
embedding_text: peace formula, God as the ultimate enjoyer and proprietor, well-wisher of all beings, attaining peace from miseries

sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
अनाश्रित: कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति य: |
स सन्न्यासी च योगी च न निरग्‍निर्न चाक्रिय: ॥ १ ॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
anāśritaḥ karma-phalaṁ kāryaṁ karma karoti yaḥ
sa sannyāsī ca yogī ca na niragnir na cākriyaḥ

meanings.english: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: One who is unattached to the fruits of his work and who works as he is obligated is in the renounced order of life, and he is the true mystic, not he who lights no fire and performs no duty.
meanings.hindi: श्रीभगवान् ने कहा: जो पुरुष अपने कर्मफल के प्रति अनासक्त है और जो अपने कर्तव्य का पालन करता है, वही संन्यासी और असली योगी है; वह नहीं जो न तो अग्नि जलाता है और न कर्म करता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo insaan results ki chinta kiye bina apni duty puri karta hai, wahi asli sanyasi aur yogi hai. Sirf kaam chhod dene se koi sanyasi nahi banta.
meanings.marathi: जो कर्माच्या फळाची अपेक्षा न ठेवता कर्तव्यकर्म करतो, तोच खरा संन्यासी आणि योगी होय. केवळ अग्नीचा त्याग करणारा किंवा निष्क्रिय राहणारा संन्यासी नव्हे.

core_meaning: Active Renunciation: True spirituality is defined by performing one's duties without self-interest, rather than abandoning responsibilities.
use_cases: focusing on quality of work instead of just the salary or reward
avoiding the 'escapist' mentality toward spiritual life
practicing karma-yoga in a corporate or professional setting
developing a sense of purpose beyond personal gain
embedding_text: unattached to fruits of work, performing obligatory duty, true sannyasi and yogi

sanskrit:
यं सन्न्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव |
न ह्यसन्न्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥ २ ॥
transliteration:
yaṁ sannyāsam iti prāhur yogaṁ taṁ viddhi pāṇḍava
na hy asannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaścana

meanings.english: What is called renunciation (sannyāsa), you should know to be the same as yoga, O son of Pāṇḍu; for no one can become a yogī without renouncing the desire for personal gratification.
meanings.hindi: हे पाण्डुपुत्र! जिसे संन्यास कहते हैं, उसे ही तुम योग (परमात्मा से मिलन) जानो; क्योंकि संकल्पों (इन्द्रिय तृप्ति की इच्छा) का त्याग किए बिना कोई भी पुरुष योगी नहीं हो सकता।
meanings.hinglish: Arjun, jise log 'Sannyas' (tyag) kehte hain, wahi asli 'Yoga' hai. Jab tak tum apne swarth aur phal (results) ki lalach nahi chhodte, tab tak tum yogi nahi ban sakte.
meanings.marathi: हे पांडवा, ज्याला संन्यास असे म्हणतात, तोच तू योग समज; कारण संकल्पांचा (फळाच्या आशेचा) त्याग केल्याशिवाय कोणीही योगी होऊ शकत नाही.

core_meaning: The Identity of Yoga and Renunciation: True 'Yoga' isn't just physical postures—it's the internal act of letting go of 'Sankalpa' (the plan for self-enjoyment). You cannot connect with the Supreme while still being obsessed with your own selfish agenda.
use_cases: realizing that 'giving up' a result is what actually allows you to focus on the work
understanding that a true developer is a 'sannyāsī' when they focus on the code's quality rather than just the credit
breaking the habit of over-planning for future rewards while ignoring present duties
finding mental freedom by dropping the 'What's in it for me?' attitude
aligning professional skills with a larger purpose to achieve effortless focus
embedding_text: renunciation is yoga, sannyasa is yoga, give up desire for self-satisfaction, sankalpa tyaga

sanskrit:
आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते |
योगारूढस्यतस्यैव शम: कारणमुच्यते ॥ ३ ॥
transliteration:
ārurukṣor muner yogaṁ karma kāraṇam ucyate
yogārūḍhasya tasyaiva śamaḥ kāraṇam ucyate

meanings.english: For one who is a neophyte in the eightfold yoga system, work is said to be the means; and for one who is already elevated in yoga, cessation of all material activities is said to be the means.
meanings.hindi: अष्टांग योग में अभी चढ़ने की इच्छा रखने वाले (आरुरुक्षु) के लिए कर्म ही साधन है; और योग में सिद्ध (योगारूढ़) हो चुके पुरुष के लिए शांति (समस्त भौतिक कार्यों का त्याग) ही साधन है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo yoga seekhna shuru kar rahe hain, unke liye 'action' rasta hai. Par jo advance level pe pahunch gaye hain, unke liye 'peace/stillness' rasta hai.
meanings.marathi: योगाभ्यासाला नुकतीच सुरुवात करणाऱ्यासाठी कर्म हेच साधन आहे; आणि जो योगारूढ झाला आहे, त्याच्यासाठी शांती (कर्मांचा अभाव) हेच साधन आहे.

core_meaning: The Yoga Ladder: Spirituality involves a progression—initially using disciplined action to stabilize the mind, and later achieving inner stillness and cessation of material disturbance.
use_cases: understanding the stages of professional or spiritual growth
using routine and work to curb a restless mind
recognizing when to move from 'doing' to 'being'
developing mental equilibrium through focused activity
transitioning to deeper meditation after mastering basic discipline
embedding_text: yoga ladder, work for neophytes, stillness for elevated yogis, progression in meditation

sanskrit:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्ज‍ते |
सर्वसङ्कल्पसन्न्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥ ४ ॥
transliteration:
yadā hi nendriyārtheṣu na karmasv anuṣajjate
sarva-saṅkalpa-sannyāsī yogārūḍhas tadocyate

meanings.english: A person is said to be elevated in yoga (yogārūḍha) when, having renounced all material desires, they neither act for sense gratification nor engage in fruitive activities.
meanings.hindi: जब कोई मनुष्य न तो इन्द्रिय-तृप्ति के लिए कार्य करता है और न ही सकाम कर्मों में फंसता है, बल्कि सभी सांसारिक संकल्पों का त्याग कर देता है, तब वह 'योगारूढ़' (योग के शिखर पर पहुँचा हुआ) कहलाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jab koi insaan maze (senses) ke liye kaam karna chhod deta hai aur results ki chinta nahi karta, tab wo sahi maayne mein 'Yog-aroodh' yaani yoga ki top level pe hota hai.
meanings.marathi: जेव्हा मनुष्य इंद्रियांच्या विषयात आणि कर्माच्या फळामध्ये आसक्त होत नाही आणि सर्व संकल्पांचा त्याग करतो, तेव्हा तो 'योगारूढ' झाला असे म्हटले जाते.

core_meaning: The Final Ascent: The 'top rung' of the yoga ladder is reached when your motivation shifts from 'What will I get?' to 'What is my service?'. At this stage, work continues, but the mental attachment to selfish results is completely cut off.
use_cases: achieving a state of 'flow' where the work itself is the reward, not the paycheck
moving beyond the need for constant validation or social media dopamine hits
transitioning from a junior mindset (task-oriented) to a master mindset (purpose-oriented)
avoiding the trap of over-analyzing every move for personal gain
finding stability during projects where the outcome is uncertain but the effort is essential
embedding_text: elevated in yoga, yogarudha, renouncing material desires, no fruitive activities

sanskrit:
उद्धरेदात्मनात्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् |
आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मन: ॥ ५ ॥
transliteration:
uddhared ātmanātmānaṁ nātmānam avasādayet
ātmaiva hy ātmano bandhur ātmaiva ripur ātmanaḥ

meanings.english: One must deliver himself with the help of his mind, and not degrade himself. The mind is the friend of the conditioned soul, and his enemy as well.
meanings.hindi: मनुष्य को चाहिए कि वह अपने मन के द्वारा अपना उद्धार करे और अपने आपको नीचे न गिरने दे। क्योंकि यह मन ही आत्मा का मित्र है और मन ही आत्मा का शत्रु भी है।
meanings.hinglish: Apne mind se khud ko lift karo, girne mat do. Tumhara mann hi tumhara sabse bada dost hai aur wahi tumhara dushman bhi.
meanings.marathi: आपल्या मनाने आपला उद्धार करावा, स्वतःची अधोगती करू नये; कारण मन हेच स्वतःचा मित्र आणि मन हेच स्वतःचा शत्रू आहे.

core_meaning: Mental Self-Reliance: The mind is a neutral tool; if trained, it saves you; if left uncontrolled, it destroys you.
use_cases: taking ownership of personal growth and mental state
training the mind to focus on productive goals
using willpower to resist harmful addictions
embedding_text: elevate self by mind, mind is friend and enemy, avoiding self-degradation

sanskrit:
बन्धुरात्मात्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जित: |
अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥ ६ ॥
transliteration:
bandhur ātmātmanas tasya yenātmaivātmanā jitaḥ
anātmanas tu śatrutve vartetātmaiva śatru-vat

meanings.english: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his mind will remain the greatest enemy.
meanings.hindi: जिसने अपने मन को जीत लिया है, उसके लिए मन सबसे अच्छा मित्र है; लेकिन जो ऐसा करने में असफल रहा है, उसका मन उसका सबसे बड़ा शत्रु बना रहेगा।
meanings.hinglish: Jisne apne mann ko jeet liya, uska mann uska sabse accha dost hai. Par jo mann ko control nahi kar paata, uska mann hi uska sabse bada dushman ban jata hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याने स्वतःच्या मनावर विजय मिळवला आहे, त्याचे मन त्याचा सर्वात मोठा मित्र असते; परंतु ज्याला मन जिंकता आले नाही, त्याचे मन शत्रूप्रमाणे त्याच्याशी वागते.

core_meaning: The Internal Ally or Adversary: The mind is a neutral tool; it becomes a 'friend' when disciplined and an 'enemy' when it dictates terms to you through lust, anger, or distractions.
use_cases: turning a restless mind into a focused tool for deep work and problem solving
recognizing that self-sabotage is just the 'enemy' mind taking control
using meditation to shift the mind from being a 'bad boss' to a 'good servant'
avoiding impulsive decisions by observing the mind's 'enemy' tendencies
building mental resilience so your inner voice encourages rather than criticizes you
embedding_text: mind is friend to one who conquered it, mind is enemy to one who failed to control it

sanskrit:
जितात्मन: प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहित: |
शीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु तथा मानापमानयो: ॥ ७ ॥
transliteration:
jitātmanaḥ praśāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ
śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ

meanings.english: For one who has conquered the mind, the Supersoul is already reached, for he has attained tranquillity. To such a man, happiness and distress, heat and cold, honor and dishonor are all the same.
meanings.hindi: जिसने मन को जीत लिया है और जो शांत हो गया है, उसने परमात्मा को प्राप्त कर लिया है। ऐसे व्यक्ति के लिए सुख-दुःख, सर्दी-गर्मी और मान-अपमान सब समान हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jisne mann jeet liya wo hamesha shant rehta hai aur bhagwan ke kareeb hota hai. Uske liye sukh-dukh, thand-garmi aur izzat-beizzati sab barabar hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याने मनावर विजय मिळवला आहे आणि जो पूर्णपणे शांत झाला आहे, त्याने परमात्मा प्राप्त केला आहे असे समजावे. अशा पुरुषाला सुख-दुःख, थंडी-उष्णता आणि मान-अपमान हे सर्व समान वाटतात.

core_meaning: Practical Samādhi: Tranquility is the evidence of spiritual success. When external dualities (like extreme weather or public opinion) stop affecting your internal peace, you are in constant union with the Divine.
use_cases: staying calm and professional even when faced with harsh criticism or dishonor
maintaining focus on long-term goals regardless of temporary 'ups' or 'downs'
developing a 'thick skin' that isn't hardened by hate, but softened by peace
practicing emotional stability during high-pressure situations or personal crises
valuing your internal state of being over the external conditions of the world
embedding_text: conquered mind attained tranquillity, dualities are the same, reached the Supersoul

sanskrit:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्त‍ात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रिय: |
युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्ट्राश्मकाञ्चन: ॥ ८ ॥
transliteration:
jñāna-vijñāna-tṛptātmā kūṭa-stho vijitendriyaḥ
yukta ity ucyate yogī sama-loṣṭrāśma-kāñcanaḥ

meanings.english: A person is established in self-realization when he is fully satisfied by virtue of acquired and realized knowledge. Such a person sees pebbles, stones or gold as the same.
meanings.hindi: जिसका मन ज्ञान तथा आत्म-साक्षात्कार से तृप्त है, जो जितेंद्रिय है, वह योगी कहलाता है। उसके लिए मिट्टी का ढेला, पत्थर और सोना सब एक समान हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Jab insaan ko real knowledge mil jati hai aur wo apni senses control kar leta hai, tab uske liye mitti ka tukda, pathar aur sona sab ek barabar hote hain.
meanings.marathi: ज्याचे मन ज्ञान आणि विज्ञानाने तृप्त आहे, जो निर्विकार आणि जितेंद्रिय आहे, असा योगी माती, दगड आणि सोने यांना समान मानतो.

core_meaning: Transcendent Equanimity: Realized knowledge leads to complete satisfaction, making one indifferent to material valuations like wealth (gold) vs commonality (stone).
use_cases: reducing the anxiety caused by material comparison
finding happiness in simple things rather than just luxury
developing a focus on 'realized' wisdom over 'book' knowledge
remaining steady during financial ups and downs
embedding_text: satisfied by realized knowledge, senses controlled, gold and stone seen as equal

sanskrit:
सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु |
साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते ॥ ९ ॥
transliteration:
suhṛn-mitrāry-udāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣya-bandhuṣu
sādhuṣv api ca pāpeṣu sama-buddhir viśiṣyate

meanings.english: A person is considered even more advanced when they regard honest well-wishers, affectionate benefactors, the neutral, mediators, the envious, friends, enemies, the pious, and the sinners all with an equal mind.
meanings.hindi: जो व्यक्ति स्वभाव से हितैषी, मित्रों, शत्रुओं, तटस्थों, मध्यस्थों, ईर्ष्यालुओं, संबंधियों, धर्मात्माओं और पापियों—इन सबके प्रति समान बुद्धि रखता है, वह अत्यंत श्रेष्ठ माना जाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Wo insaan sabse zyada advanced hai jo doston, dushmanon, anjanon, acche logon aur bure logon—sabko ek hi nazar se dekhta hai aur kisi ke liye bhed-bhav nahi rakhta.
meanings.marathi: जो पुरुष सुहृद, मित्र, शत्रू, तटस्थ, मध्यस्थ, द्वेषपात्र, बांधव, साधू आणि पापी या सर्वांच्या ठिकाणी समबुद्धी ठेवतो, तो श्रेष्ठ समजला जातो.

core_meaning: Universal Neutrality: The peak of spiritual evolution is 'Sama-buddhi'—the ability to look past social labels, moral standings, and personal history to see the same core essence in everyone, regardless of how they treat you.
use_cases: maintaining professional ethics even when working with people you personally dislike
avoiding 'office politics' by being equally fair to allies and competitors
cultivating a non-judgmental attitude toward people from different backgrounds or life choices
staying emotionally stable when faced with both public praise and online trolling
seeing the human element in everyone, beyond the labels of 'good' or 'bad'
embedding_text: equal mind toward friends enemies neutrals sinners and pious, sama-buddhir visisyate

sanskrit:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थित: |
एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रह: ॥ १० ॥
transliteration:
yogī yuñjīta satatam ātmānaṁ rahasi sthitaḥ
ekākī yata-cittātmā nirāśīr aparigrahaḥ

meanings.english: A transcendentalist should always engage his body, mind and self in relationship with the Supreme; he should live alone in a secluded place, control his mind, and be free from desires and possessiveness.
meanings.hindi: योगी को चाहिए कि वह अपने शरीर, मन तथा आत्मा को निरंतर परमेश्वर में लगाए; वह अकेले एकांत स्थान में रहे और अपनी इच्छाओं तथा संचय की वृत्ति (possessiveness) से मुक्त हो।
meanings.hinglish: Yogi ko akele me, bina kisi lalach aur possession ke, apna poora dhyan God me lagana chahiye.
meanings.marathi: योग्याने एकांतात राहून, चित्त आणि शरीर स्वाधीन ठेवून, आशारहित आणि संग्रहशून्य होऊन सतत परमात्म्याशी स्वतःला जोडून ठेवावे.

core_meaning: The Environment of Focus: Meditation requires mental and physical seclusion, eliminating the 'noise' of desires and the burden of hoarding material things.
use_cases: practicing daily meditation or deep work sessions in quiet places
decluttering one's physical and digital life (digital minimalism)
learning to be comfortable in one's own company
reducing the stress of always wanting more (non-possessiveness)
developing an 'inner sanctuary' in a busy world
embedding_text: live in seclusion, control mind, free from possessiveness and desires, engage with Supreme

sanskrit:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मन: |
नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥ ११ ॥
तत्रैकाग्रं मन: कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रिय: |
उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥ १२ ॥
transliteration:
śucau deśe pratiṣṭhāpya sthiram āsanam ātmanaḥ
nāty-ucchritaṁ nāti-nīcaṁ cailājina-kuśottaram
tatraikāgraṁ manaḥ kṛtvā yata-cittendriya-kriyaḥ
upaviśyāsane yuñjyād yogam ātma-viśuddhaye

meanings.english: To practice yoga, one should go to a secluded place and should lay kuśa grass on the ground and then cover it with a deerskin and a soft cloth. The seat should be neither too high nor too low. The yogī should then sit on it very firmly and practice yoga to purify the heart by controlling his mind, senses and activities.
meanings.hindi: योगाभ्यास के लिए पवित्र स्थान में, कुशा, मृगछाला और वस्त्र से ढका हुआ आसन न बहुत ऊँचा हो न बहुत नीचा। वहाँ बैठकर, मन और इन्द्रियों की क्रियाओं को वश में करते हुए, मन को एकाग्र कर अंतःकरण की शुद्धि के लिए योग का अभ्यास करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Yoga ke liye ek saaf aur shant jagah chuno. Tera aasan na zyada uncha ho na neecha. Wahan baith kar, apne dimaag aur senses ko control karke, man ki safai ke liye focus ke saath practice karo.
meanings.marathi: योगाभ्यासासाठी पवित्र ठिकाणी दर्भ, मृगाजिन आणि वस्त्र यावर बसून, मन आणि इंद्रिये स्वाधीन ठेवून, अंतःकरण शुद्ध करण्यासाठी योगाचा सराव करावा.

core_meaning: Setting the Stage: Spiritual progress requires a stable, clean, and distraction-free environment to facilitate deep mental concentration and inner purification.
use_cases: creating a dedicated 'focus zone' at home for meditation or work
understanding the impact of physical surroundings on mental clarity
prioritizing hygiene and order as foundations for focus
using physical posture and environment to signal the brain to stay calm
organizing a workspace to minimize visual and audible noise
embedding_text: yoga environment, secluded place, seat not too high or low, purify heart by controlling mind

sanskrit:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिर: |
सम्प्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥ १३ ॥
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थित: |
मन: संयम्य मच्चित्त‍ो युक्त आसीत मत्पर: ॥ १४ ॥
transliteration:
samaṁ kāya-śiro-grīvaṁ dhārayann acalaṁ sthiraḥ
samprekṣya nāsikāgraṁ svaṁ diśaś cānavalokayan
praśāntātmā vigata-bhīr brahmacāri-vrate sthitaḥ
manaḥ saṁyamya mac-citto yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ

meanings.english: One should hold one’s body, neck and head erect in a straight line and stare steadily at the tip of the nose. Thus, with an unagitated, subdued mind, devoid of fear, and completely self-controlled, one should meditate upon Me within the heart.
meanings.hindi: शरीर, गर्दन और सिर को सीधा रखते हुए, अपनी नासिका के अगले भाग पर दृष्टि टिकाकर, शांत और निर्भय होकर, मन को पूरी तरह वश में रखते हुए मेरा चिंतन करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Apni body, gale aur sir ko ek seedhi line me rakho. Bina dar ke, shant mann se apni saanson aur God pe focus karo. Dimaag ko bhatakne mat do.
meanings.marathi: शरीर, मान आणि डोके सरळ व ताठ धरून, कोठेही न पाहता नाकाच्या शेंड्यावर लक्ष केंद्रित करून, निर्भय व शांत मनाने माझे ध्यान करावे.

core_meaning: Alignment of Form and Spirit: Correct physical posture and sensory withdrawal (looking inward) are essential to achieving a state of fearlessness and total absorption in the higher self.
use_cases: using upright posture to boost confidence and alertness
practicing breathwork and fixed gaze to reduce anxiety
overcoming fear by centering the mind on a higher purpose
developing the 'stillness' required for complex problem-solving
aligning physical health with mental and spiritual goals
embedding_text: body neck head erect, staring at tip of nose, devoid of fear, meditating on Supreme

sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी नियतमानस: |
शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥ १५ ॥
transliteration:
yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī niyata-mānasaḥ
śāntiṁ nirvāṇa-paramāṁ mat-saṁsthām adhigacchati

meanings.english: Thus practicing constant control of the body, mind and activities, the mystic transcendentalist, his mind regulated, attains to the kingdom of God by cessation of material existence.
meanings.hindi: इस प्रकार शरीर, मन और कर्मों पर निरंतर नियंत्रण रखते हुए, संयमित मन वाला योगी परम शांति को प्राप्त करता है, जो भौतिक अस्तित्व से परे आध्यात्मिक आकाश में स्थित है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo hamesha apne body aur mind ko control me rakhta hai, use wo ultimate peace milti hai jo is duniya ki tensions se upar hai.
meanings.marathi: अशा प्रकारे निरंतर योगाभ्यास करून ज्याचे मन ताब्यात आले आहे, असा योगी परम शांती आणि मोक्ष प्राप्त करतो.

core_meaning: The Ultimate Result: Consistent self-regulation leads to 'Nirvana-paramam'—the highest peace and liberation from the cycle of suffering.
use_cases: realizing that inner peace is the end-goal of all discipline
finding a sense of 'home' within oneself
understanding that spiritual success is a matter of constant practice
moving beyond temporary solutions to permanent mental stability
attaining a state of flow that feels effortless and peaceful
embedding_text: regulated mind attains peace, kingdom of God, cessation of material existence

sanskrit:
नात्यश्न‍तस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्न‍त: |
न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥ १६ ॥
transliteration:
nāty-aśnatas tu yogo ’sti na caikāntam anaśnataḥ
nāty-aśnatas tu yogo ’sti na caikāntam anaśnataḥ

meanings.english: There is no possibility of one’s becoming a yogī, O Arjuna, if one eats too much or eats too little, sleeps too much or does not sleep enough.
meanings.hindi: हे अर्जुन! जो बहुत अधिक खाता है या बहुत कम खाता है, जो बहुत अधिक सोता है या पर्याप्त नहीं सोता, उसके लिए योग में सफलता की कोई संभावना नहीं है।
meanings.hinglish: Arjun, jo bohot zyada khata hai ya ekdum nahi khata, jo bohot sota hai ya bilkul nahi sota, wo kabhi yogi nahi ban sakta.
meanings.marathi: हे अर्जुना, जो खूप खातो किंवा जो अजिबात खात नाही, जो खूप झोपतो किंवा जो सतत जागतो, त्याला योग साध्य होत नाही.

core_meaning: The Path of Moderation: Extremes in biological habits (eating/sleeping) disturb the nervous system, making deep meditation or mental growth impossible.
use_cases: correcting irregular sleep cycles for better cognitive function
avoiding binge-eating or extreme fasting for emotional balance
understanding that health is the prerequisite for spirituality
optimizing physical energy for long study or work sessions
avoiding 'burnout' caused by over-working and under-sleeping
embedding_text: no yoga for extremes, eating or sleeping too much or too little, balance required

sanskrit:
युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु |
युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दु:खहा ॥ १७ ॥
transliteration:
yuktāhāra-vihārasya yukta-ceṣṭasya karmasu
yukta-svapnāvabodhasya yogo bhavati duḥkha-hā

meanings.english: He who is regulated in his habits of eating, sleeping, recreation and work can mitigate all material pains by practicing the yoga system.
meanings.hindi: जो खाने, सोने, मनोरंजन तथा कर्म करने की आदतों में संयमित रहता है, वह योगाभ्यास के द्वारा समस्त दुखों को नष्ट कर सकता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo khane-peene, ghoomne, kaam karne aur sone me ek balance rakhta hai, uske saare dukh is yoga se door ho jate hain.
meanings.marathi: ज्याचा आहार, विहार आणि झोपणे-जागणे नियमित आहे, अशा माणसाचा योग सर्व दुःखांचा नाश करणारा असतो.

core_meaning: Equilibrium as Therapy: A structured lifestyle is not just a rule, but a 'pain-killer' (duḥkha-hā) that prevents mental and physical suffering.
use_cases: balancing 'hustle' culture with proper recreation and rest
using 'lifestyle medicine' to improve mood and focus
building a sustainable career that doesn't sacrifice health
finding inner harmony through predictable daily habits
embedding_text: regulated habits in eating sleeping work and recreation, yoga removes misery

sanskrit:
यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते |
निस्पृह: सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥ १८ ॥
transliteration:
yadā viniyataṁ cittam ātmany evāvatiṣṭhate
nispṛhaḥ sarva-kāmebhyo yukta ity ucyate tadā

meanings.english: When the yogī, by practice of yoga, disciplines his mental activities and becomes situated in transcendence – devoid of all material desires – he is said to be well established in yoga.
meanings.hindi: जब योगी योगाभ्यास द्वारा अपने मानसिक कार्यकलापों को अनुशासित कर लेता है और समस्त भौतिक इच्छाओं से रहित होकर आत्म-स्वरूप में स्थित हो जाता है, तब वह योग में 'युक्त' (सिद्ध) कहलाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jab insaan apne dimaag ko itna control kar le ki uski koi fati-purani material wish na rahe aur wo bas shanti me tik jaye, tab wo perfect yogi hai.
meanings.marathi: जेव्हा अत्यंत आवरलेले मन केवळ आत्म्याच्या ठिकाणी स्थिर होते आणि सर्व भोगांची इच्छा संपते, तेव्हा तो पुरुष योगयुक्त म्हटला जातो.

core_meaning: The definition of Mastery: Being 'Yoga-yukta' means reaching a state where the mind is so disciplined that it no longer hungers for external gratification.
use_cases: finding independence from external validation or praise
reaching a state of deep contentment without buying new things
disciplining the mind to stay with one idea or task
becoming 'undisturbable' by external marketing or FOMO
achieving true autonomy over one's thoughts
embedding_text: disciplined mental activities, devoid of desires, established in yoga

sanskrit:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता |
योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मन: ॥ १९ ॥
transliteration:
yathā dīpo nivāta-stho neṅgate sopamā smṛtā
yogino yata-cittasya yuñjato yogam ātmanaḥ

meanings.english: As a lamp in a windless place does not waver, so the transcendentalist, whose mind is controlled, remains always steady in his meditation on the transcendent Self.
meanings.hindi: जिस प्रकार वायु-रहित स्थान में दीपक की लौ डगमगाती नहीं है, उसी प्रकार संयमित मन वाले योगी की बुद्धि आत्म-साक्षात्कार में सदैव स्थिर रहती है।
meanings.hinglish: Jaise band kamre me diye ki lau hilti nahi hai, waise hi ek controlled mind wala insaan bina kisi bhatkaav ke focus rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: वाऱ्याविना असलेल्या जागी दिव्याची ज्योत जशी स्थिर राहते, तसेच आत्मध्यान करणाऱ्या योग्याचे मन स्थिर असते.

core_meaning: Unshakable Focus: This famous metaphor illustrates the ultimate state of concentration—a mind so steady that even the 'wind' of thoughts cannot make it flicker.
use_cases: maintaining total focus during a critical exam or presentation
staying emotionally stable during a personal crisis
developing deep meditative states of awareness
embedding_text: lamp in windless place, controlled mind remains steady in meditation

sanskrit:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया |
यत्र चैवात्मनात्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥ २० ॥
सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्‍बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् |
वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वत: ॥ २१ ॥
यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं तत: |
यस्मिन्स्थितो न दु:खेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥ २२ ॥
तं विद्याद्दु:खसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् ॥ २३ ॥
transliteration:
yatroparamate cittaṁ niruddhaṁ yoga-sevayā...
taṁ vidyād duḥkha-saṁyoga-viyogaṁ yoga-saṁjñitam

meanings.english: In the stage of perfection called samādhi, one's mind is completely restrained from material activities. One rejoices in the Self, experiencing boundless transcendental happiness. Once established, one never departs from the truth, considers no gain greater, and is never shaken even by the greatest difficulty. This is actual freedom from all miseries.
meanings.hindi: योग के अभ्यास से जब चित्त शांत हो जाता है, तब मनुष्य शुद्ध बुद्धि द्वारा अपने आप को देखता है और अपने आप में ही संतुष्ट रहता है। उस अवस्था में वह असीम सुख का अनुभव करता है और बड़े से बड़े दुःख से भी विचलित नहीं होता। इसे ही दुखों के संयोग से वियोग (योग) कहते हैं।
meanings.hinglish: Samadhi wo stage hai jahan mann bilkul shant ho jata hai. Wahan insaan ko aisi khushi milti hai jo senses se pare hai. Is stage pe pahunch kar bade se bada dukh bhi insaan ko hila nahi sakta.
meanings.marathi: योगाच्या अभ्यासाने चित्त निरुद्ध होते, तेव्हा मनुष्य स्वतःच्या अंतःकरणात स्वतःला पाहून संतुष्ट होतो. त्या अवस्थेत त्याला इंद्रियांच्या पलीकडचे सुख मिळते आणि मोठ्या दुःखातही तो डगमगत नाही.

core_meaning: The Definition of Yoga: Yoga is defined here as 'the disconnection from the connection with misery.' It is a state of absolute mental stillness (Samādhi) where internal joy is so profound that external chaos becomes irrelevant.
use_cases: building resilience against 'burnout' by finding an internal source of peace
maintaining focus during a high-stakes engineering project despite technical failures
realizing that 'happiness' is a state of the soul, not a result of material gain
remaining calm during personal crises or major life changes
attaining a state of 'flow' where distractions simply cease to exist
embedding_text: samadhi trance, freedom from misery, boundless happiness, unshakable in difficulty

sanskrit:
स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा |
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषत: |
मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्तत: ॥ २४ ॥
transliteration:
sa niścayena yoktavyo yogo ’nirviṇṇa-cetasā
saṅkalpa-prabhavān kāmāṁs tyaktvā sarvān aśeṣataḥ
manasaivendriya-grāmaṁ viniyamya samantataḥ

meanings.english: One should practice yoga with firm determination and faith, without becoming discouraged. One must abandon all material desires born of mental speculation and control the senses from all sides by the mind.
meanings.hindi: मनुष्य को चाहिए कि वह बिना विचलित हुए पूरे संकल्प और विश्वास के साथ योग का अभ्यास करे। उसे मन से उत्पन्न होने वाली सभी इच्छाओं को पूरी तरह त्याग देना चाहिए और मन के द्वारा इन्द्रियों को सभी ओर से वश में करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Pure bharose aur determination ke saath yoga karo, haar mat maano. Apne dimaag ki banai hui saari lalach chhod do aur mann se senses ko control karo.
meanings.marathi: निश्चयाने आणि न डगमगता योगाचा अभ्यास करावा. संकल्पामुळे निर्माण होणाऱ्या सर्व वासनांचा त्याग करून मनाने इंद्रियांना सर्व बाजूंनी आवरून धरावे.

core_meaning: The Sparrow's Determination: Success in any discipline requires 'utsāha' (enthusiasm) and 'niścaya' (conviction). Even if progress is slow, the yogi must persist like the sparrow trying to dry up the ocean.
use_cases: persisting through difficult academic subjects or long coding marathons
avoiding 'mental burnout' by maintaining long-term vision
regulating distractions (social media, habits) using the power of the mind
building a career from scratch with patience and faith in the process
refusing to be discouraged when results are delayed
embedding_text: practice with determination, abandon mental speculation, control senses with mind

sanskrit:
शनै: शनैरुपरमेद्‍बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया |
आत्मसंस्थं मन: कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥ २५ ॥
transliteration:
śanaiḥ śanair uparamed buddhyā dhṛti-gṛhītayā
ātma-saṁsthaṁ manaḥ kṛtvā na kiñcid api cintayet

meanings.english: Gradually, step by step, one should become situated in trance by means of intelligence sustained by full conviction, and thus the mind should be fixed on the Self alone and should think of nothing else.
meanings.hindi: धैर्य और बुद्धि के द्वारा धीरे-धीरे अभ्यास करते हुए मन को परमात्मा में स्थित करना चाहिए और फिर किसी अन्य वस्तु का चिन्तन नहीं करना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Dhire-dhire, step-by-step apni akal aur patience se mann ko soul me fix karo aur kuch mat socho.
meanings.marathi: धैर्याने युक्त असलेल्या बुद्धीच्या साहाय्याने हळूहळू शांती मिळवावी आणि मनाला आत्म्यामध्ये स्थिर करून दुसरे काहीही विचारू नये.

core_meaning: The Gradual Process: Mental mastery doesn't happen overnight. It is a 'śanaiḥ śanaiḥ' (slow and steady) process of using the intelligence to pull the mind back into focus until it becomes still.
use_cases: practicing focused meditation sessions, starting small and increasing daily
learning complex skills (like Blockchain or DSA) through consistent, small steps
calming an overactive mind by focusing on one thing at a time
understanding that lasting change is evolutionary, not revolutionary
achieving 'deep focus' by slowly weeding out background thoughts
embedding_text: step by step practice, mind fixed on self, think of nothing else

sanskrit:
यतो यतो निश्चलति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् |
ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥ २६ ॥
transliteration:
yato yato niścalati manaś cañcalam asthiram
tatas tato niyamyaitad ātmany eva vaśaṁ nayet

meanings.english: From wherever the mind wanders due to its flickering and unsteady nature, one must certainly withdraw it and bring it back under the control of the Self.
meanings.hindi: यह चंचल और अस्थिर मन जहाँ-जहाँ भटके, वहाँ-वहाँ से इसे खींचकर वापस आत्मा के वश में लाना चाहिए।
meanings.hinglish: Ye chanchal mann jahan bhi bhatke, wahan se ise pakad kar wapas lao aur apne control me rakho.
meanings.marathi: हे चंचल आणि अस्थिर मन ज्या ज्या कारणाने भटकते, त्या त्या कारणापासून याला आवरून आत्म्यामध्ये स्थिर करावे.

core_meaning: The Infinite Loop of Discipline: The nature of the mind is to wander ('cañcalam'). The job of the practitioner is not to stop the wandering immediately, but to develop the habit of pulling it back, every single time it drifts.
use_cases: redirecting focus when your mind drifts during a lecture or coding session
not getting frustrated with yourself when your mind wanders during meditation
catching yourself when you fall into 'daydreaming' about the future or past
developing the mental 'muscle' of attention through repeated correction
managing ADHD-like symptoms by gently returning to the task at hand
embedding_text: mind wanders bring it back, flickering unsteady mind, bring under control of self

sanskrit:
प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् |
उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥ २७ ॥
transliteration:
praśānta-manasaṁ hy enaṁ yoginaṁ sukham uttamam
upaiti śānta-rajasaṁ brahma-bhūtam akalmaṣam

meanings.english: The yogī whose mind is fixed on Me attains the highest perfection of transcendental happiness. He is beyond the mode of passion, realizes his identity with the Supreme, and is freed from all reactions to past deeds.
meanings.hindi: जिस योगी का मन मुझमें स्थिर है, वह निश्चय ही परम सुख को प्राप्त करता है। उसकी रजोगुणी वृत्तियाँ शांत हो जाती हैं, वह ब्रह्म के साथ अपनी गुणात्मक एकता का अनुभव करता है और सभी पापों से मुक्त हो जाता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jis yogi ka mann mujhme (Krishna) laga hai, use sabse badi khushi milti hai. Uska gussa aur lalach shant ho jata hai aur wo paapon se mukt ho jata hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याचे मन शांत झाले आहे, ज्याचा रजोगुण शांत झाला आहे आणि जो पापापासून मुक्त झाला आहे, अशा योगीला सर्वोच्च सुख प्राप्त होते.

core_meaning: The Peace Beyond Passion: When the mind stops racing (Rajo-guna) and becomes 'Praśānta' (peaceful), the yogi experiences 'Brahma-bhūta'—a state of divine purity where the weight of past mistakes (Karma) is lifted.
use_cases: clearing the 'cache' of past regrets and failures to focus on the present
experiencing the 'high' of spiritual connection that is superior to physical pleasure
achieving a state of mental 'purity' required for creative breakthroughs
finding deep emotional healing through spiritual absorption
embedding_text: highest happiness for peaceful mind, beyond passion, brahma-bhuta

sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदात्मानं योगी विगतकल्मष: |
सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्न‍ुते ॥ २८ ॥
transliteration:
yuñjann evaṁ sadātmānaṁ yogī vigata-kalmaṣaḥ
sukhena brahma-saṁsparśam atyantam sukham aśnute

meanings.english: Thus the self-controlled yogī, constantly engaged in yoga practice, becomes free from all material contamination and achieves the highest stage of perfect happiness in transcendental loving service to the Lord.
meanings.hindi: इस प्रकार निरंतर योगाभ्यास में लगा हुआ संयमी योगी समस्त भौतिक कल्मषों से मुक्त होकर परमात्मा के साथ निरंतर संपर्क (ब्रह्म-संस्पर्श) का असीम सुख भोगता है।
meanings.hinglish: Hamesha yoga practice karne wala yogi har tarah ki gandagi (contamination) se free ho jata hai aur bhagwan ke saath jud kar sabse bada sukh pata hai.
meanings.marathi: अशा प्रकारे सतत योगाभ्यास करणारा योगी पापांपासून मुक्त होतो आणि ब्रह्माच्या स्पर्शाने अत्यंत सुख भोगतो.

core_meaning: Brahma-Saṁsparśa: The 'Touch of the Absolute.' By consistent practice, the yogi maintains a continuous spiritual connection that acts as a permanent battery for inner joy, making them immune to the 'pollution' of worldly stress.
use_cases: maintaining a 'spiritual routine' that keeps you grounded throughout a busy week
finding a sense of 'flow' that lasts beyond a single task and becomes a lifestyle
realizing that consistency in practice is the key to deep spiritual results
cleansing your mental 'browser history' of negative influences
living in a state of constant 'oneness' with your higher purpose
embedding_text: constant yoga practice, free from contamination, brahma-samsparsa

sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि |
ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शन: ॥ २९ ॥
transliteration:
sarva-bhūta-stham ātmānaṁ sarva-bhūtāni cātmani
īkṣate yoga-yuktātmā sarvatra sama-darśanaḥ

meanings.english: A true yogī observes Me in all beings and also sees every being in Me. Indeed, the self-realized person sees the same Supreme Lord everywhere.
meanings.hindi: एक सच्चा योगी सभी प्राणियों में मुझ परमात्मा को देखता है और सभी प्राणियों को मुझमें देखता है। वास्तव में, आत्म-साक्षात्कारी व्यक्ति हर जगह एक ही परमेश्वर को देखता है।
meanings.hinglish: Sacha yogi har kisi me bhagwan ko dekhta hai aur bhagwan me sabko dekhta hai. Wo har taraf sirf ek hi truth dekhta hai.
meanings.marathi: योगामध्ये स्थिर झालेला पुरुष सर्व भूतांमध्ये मला पाहतो आणि सर्व भूते माझ्यात पाहतो. तो सर्वत्र सम दृष्टीने पाहतो.

core_meaning: The Vision of Equality: This is 'Sama-darśanaḥ'. The advanced yogi no longer sees friends, enemies, or different species—they see the same 'Supersoul' residing in the heart of a dog, a saint, or a criminal.
use_cases: overcoming prejudices and biases by seeing the common humanity in everyone
developing deep empathy for animals and the environment
maintaining calm during social conflicts by recognizing the divine spark in 'the other'
approaching user experience design by seeing the 'soul' or human need behind the screen
practicing inclusive leadership that treats everyone with equal respect
embedding_text: see Me in all beings, every being in Me, sama-darsanah vision of equality

sanskrit:
यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति |
तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्याति ॥ ३० ॥
transliteration:
yo māṁ paśyati sarvatra sarvaṁ ca mayi paśyati
tasyāhaṁ na praṇaśyāmi sa ca me na praṇaśyati

meanings.english: For one who sees Me everywhere and sees everything in Me, I am never lost, nor is he ever lost to Me.
meanings.hindi: जो मुझे हर जगह देखता है और सब कुछ मुझमें देखता है, उसके लिए मैं कभी ओझल नहीं होता और वह मेरे लिए कभी ओझल नहीं होता।
meanings.hinglish: Jo mujhe har jagah dekhta hai, main uske liye kabhi gayab nahi hota aur wo hamesha meri nazar me rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: जो मला सर्वत्र पाहतो आणि सर्व काही माझ्या ठिकाणी पाहतो, त्याच्यासाठी मी कधीही नाहीसा होत नाही आणि तो माझ्यासाठी कधीही नाहीसा होत नाही.

core_meaning: The Reciprocal Bond: When you train your vision to see the Divine in every atom of the universe, the Divine 'sees' you back. It is a promise of eternal protection and presence—the ultimate insurance for the soul.
use_cases: feeling a sense of 'divine companionship' even when physically alone
finding meaning in the smallest details of nature and daily life
erasing the fear of abandonment or being 'lost' in the vast universe
turning every action into a conversation with the Supreme
experiencing constant 'awareness' that prevents unethical choices
embedding_text: never lost to Me, sees Me everywhere, intimate relationship with the Lord

sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थित: |
सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥ ३१ ॥
transliteration:
sarva-bhūta-sthitaṁ yo māṁ bhajaty ekatvam āsthitaḥ
sarvathā vartamāno ’pi sa yogī mayi वर्तते

meanings.english: Such a yogī, who engages in the worshipful service of the Supersoul, knowing that I and the Supersoul are one, remains always in Me in all circumstances.
meanings.hindi: जो योगी सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में स्थित मुझ परमात्मा की भक्ति करता है और यह जानता है कि मैं और परमात्मा एक ही हैं, वह हर प्रकार की परिस्थिति में रहते हुए भी मुझमें ही निवास करता है।
meanings.hinglish: Jo yogi sabke dilon mein rehne wale bhagwan ko mujhse alag nahi samajhta aur meri bhakti karta hai, wo chahe kuch bhi kaam kar raha ho, wo hamesha mere saath hi rehta hai.
meanings.marathi: जो योगी सर्व भूतांच्या ठिकाणी असलेल्या मला एकात्मभावाने भजतो, तो सर्व प्रकारे वागत असला तरी माझ्यातच राहतो.

core_meaning: Universal Oneness in Action: Spiritual life isn't just about sitting in a cave. If you recognize the Divine in everyone and act with that awareness, your daily tasks become a form of worship.
use_cases: finding spiritual flow in technical work
realizing you don't need to quit your life to stay connected to purpose
embedding_text: yogī worships Supersoul in all beings, remains in Me in all circumstances

sanskrit:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन |
सुखं वा यदि वा du:खं स योगी परमो मत: ॥ ३२ ॥
transliteration:
ātmaupamyena sarvatra samaṁ paśyati yo ’rjuna
sukhaṁ vā yadi vā duḥkhaṁ sa yogī paramo mataḥ

meanings.english: He is a perfect yogī who, by comparison to his own self, sees the true equality of all beings, in both their happiness and their distress, O Arjuna!
meanings.hindi: हे अर्जुन! वह पूर्ण योगी है जो दूसरों के सुख और दुःख को अपने सुख-दुःख के समान समझकर सभी प्राणियों को बराबरी की दृष्टि से देखता है।
meanings.hinglish: Arjun, asli yogi wo hai jo dusron ke dukh-sukh ko bilkul apna samajhta hai. Wo sabko barabar maanta hai.
meanings.marathi: हे अर्जुना, जो आपल्याप्रमाणेच सर्वत्र सुख किंवा दुःख सारखेच पाहतो, तो योगी सर्वश्रेष्ठ मानला जातो.

core_meaning: The Peak of Empathy: 'Ātmaupamyena' means using yourself as the yardstick. If pain hurts you, it hurts others. A perfect yogi feels the world's pain as their own and works to fix it.
use_cases: helping others because you feel their struggle as your own
avoiding actions that you wouldn't want done to you
embedding_text: perfect yogi sees equality by comparing with self, feels others happiness and distress

sanskrit:
योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्त: साम्येन मधुसूदन |
एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात्स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥ ३३ ॥
transliteration:
yo ’yaṁ yogas tvayā proktaḥ sāmyena madhusūdana
etasyāhaṁ na paśyāmi cañcalatvāt sthitiṁ sthirām

meanings.english: Arjuna said: O Madhusūdana, the system of yoga which You have summarized appears impractical and unendurable to me, for the mind is restless and unsteady.
meanings.hindi: अर्जुन ने कहा: हे मधुसूदन! आपने जिस योग पद्धति का वर्णन किया है, वह मुझे अव्यावहारिक लगती है, क्योंकि मन अत्यंत चंचल और अस्थिर है।
meanings.hinglish: Arjun ne kaha: Krishna, ye jo yoga aapne bataya, ye mujhe impractical lag raha hai kyunki mann bahut zyada chanchal hai.
meanings.marathi: अर्जुन म्हणाला, हे मधुसूदना, आपण जो हा समत्वरूपी योग सांगितला आहे, मन चंचल असल्यामुळे मला त्याची स्थिती स्थिर वाटत नाही.

core_meaning: The Reality Check: Even a legendary warrior like Arjuna admits that traditional meditation is hard because the mind won't sit still. This validates the struggle of every modern person trying to focus.
use_cases: acknowledging that meditation is naturally difficult
being honest about your internal mental chaos
validating the feeling of being overwhelmed by a restless mind
embedding_text: Arjuna says yoga is impractical due to restless mind

sanskrit:
चञ्चलं हि मन: कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम् |
तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥ ३४ ॥
transliteration:
cañcalaṁ hi manaḥ kṛṣṇa pramāthi balavad dṛḍham
tasyāhaṁ nigraham manye vāyor iva su-duṣkaram

meanings.english: The mind is restless, turbulent, obstinate and very strong, O Kṛṣṇa, and to subdue it, I think, is more difficult than controlling the wind.
meanings.hindi: हे कृष्ण! यह मन बहुत चंचल, अशांत, जिद्दी और बलवान है। मुझे इसे वश में करना हवा को रोकने से भी ज्यादा कठिन लगता है।
meanings.hinglish: Krishna, ye dimaag itna chanchal aur ziddi hai ki ise control karna hawa ko mutthi mein pakadne jaisa mushkil hai.
meanings.marathi: कारण हे कृष्णा, मन मोठे चंचल, प्रमथनशील, प्रबळ आणि हट्टी आहे; त्याला आवरणे वाऱ्याला आवरण्याप्रमाणे मला अत्यंत कठीण वाटते.

core_meaning: The Power of the Mind: The mind isn't just 'busy'; it is 'turbulent' and 'obstinate.' Controlling it is compared to catching the wind—nearly impossible without a specialized technique.
use_cases: understanding why 'willpower' alone often fails against deep habits
recognizing the mental pressure of high-stress environments
identifying the 'turbulent' nature of thoughts during anxiety
embedding_text: mind is restless turbulent strong obstinate, difficult to control like wind

sanskrit:
असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलम् |
अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥ ३५ ॥
transliteration:
asaṁśayaṁ mahā-bāho mano durnigrahaṁ calam
abhyāsena tu kaunteya vairāgyeṇa ca gṛhyate

meanings.english: Lord Kṛṣṇa said: O mighty-armed son of Kuntī, it is undoubtedly very difficult to curb the restless mind, but it is possible by suitable practice and by detachment.
meanings.hindi: भगवान कृष्ण ने कहा: हे महाबाहु! इसमें कोई शक नहीं कि मन चंचल है और इसे वश में करना कठिन है, लेकिन अभ्यास और वैराग्य के द्वारा इसे वश में किया जा सकता है।
meanings.hinglish: Krishna ne kaha: Isme koi shak nahi ki mann bhatakta hai, par 'Practice' aur 'Detachment' se ise control kiya ja sakta hai.
meanings.marathi: श्रीभगवान म्हणाले, हे महाबाहो, मन चंचल असून त्याला आवरणे कठीण आहे यात शंका नाही; परंतु हे कौन्तेया, अभ्यासाने आणि वैराग्याने ते आवरता येते.

core_meaning: The Two Pillars: Kṛṣṇa gives the solution. 1. Abhyāsa (Practice): Relentless repetition of focus. 2. Vairāgya (Detachment): Stopping the mind from wanting things that distract it.
use_cases: using consistent daily effort to master a new coding language
practicing digital minimalism to reduce mental distractions
retraining the mind to return to the present moment
embedding_text: mind is difficult to curb but possible by practice and detachment

sanskrit:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मति: |
वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्‍तुमुपायत: ॥ ३६ ॥
transliteration:
asaṁyatātmanā yogo duṣprāpa iti me matiḥ
vaśyātmanā tu yatatā śakyo ’vāptum upāyataḥ

meanings.english: For one whose mind is unbridled, self-realization is difficult work. But he whose mind is controlled and who strives by appropriate means is assured of success. That is My opinion.
meanings.hindi: जिसका मन वश में नहीं है, उसके लिए योग कठिन है। लेकिन जिसने मन को जीत लिया है और जो सही साधनों से प्रयास करता है, उसे सफलता निश्चित ही मिलती है।
meanings.hinglish: Jiska mann control mein nahi hai, uske liye success mushkil hai. Par jo sahi tarike se mehnat karta hai, wo jeet jata hai.
meanings.marathi: ज्याचे मन स्वाधीन नाही, त्याला योग प्राप्त होणे कठीण आहे; पण ज्याचे मन स्वाधीन आहे, अशा प्रयत्नशील मनुष्याला उपायाने तो प्राप्त होऊ शकतो.

core_meaning: The Master of Success: Success isn't random. It’s a mechanical outcome of mental control and using the right 'tools' or 'means' (Upāyataḥ).
use_cases: prioritizing mental discipline before executing a complex project
trusting in a proven system rather than just brute-force effort
understanding that internal balance leads to external victory
embedding_text: self-realization difficult for unbridled mind, success for controlled mind

sanskrit:
अयति: श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्च‍‍लितमानस: |
अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥ ३७ ॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca: ayatiḥ śraddhayopeto yogāc calita-mānasaḥ
